def band_result(burstkey, band):
    tel = "chime" if band=="C" else "dsa"
    fit,npz,vb=sources(burstkey)
    d=json.load(open(fit)); p={k:v["median"] for k,v in d["percentiles"].items()}
    perr={k:(abs(v.get("err_minus",0.))+abs(v.get("err_plus",0.)))/2 for k,v in d["percentiles"].items()}
    z=np.load(npz)
    t=np.asarray(z[f"time{band}"],float); freq=np.asarray(z[f"freq{band}"],float)
    data=np.asarray(z[f"data{band}"],float); noise=np.asarray(z[f"noise{band}"],float)
    dt=float(np.diff(t)[0])
    beta=_beta_for(d,band)
    m=FRBModel(t, freq, data=data, dm_init=_cfg_dm_init(burstkey,tel),
               df_MHz=NATIVE_DF[tel], beta=beta, noise_std=noise)
    pscat,tau,beta,n=_params(d,p,band,freq)
    pintr=[replace(pp,tau_1ghz=0.0) for pp in pscat]
    Ks=np.stack([m(replace(pp,c0=1.,gamma=0.),"M3") for pp in pscat])   # (N,F,T)
    Ki=np.stack([m(replace(pp,c0=1.,gamma=0.),"M3") for pp in pintr])
    npz_model=np.asarray(z[f"model{band}"],float)
    # Recover gains by OLS onto the AUTHORITATIVE stored model, so the scattered
    # reconstruction reproduces the published dump exactly; matched gains then
    # define the tau->0 intrinsic profile. Fall back to data if the stored model
    # is flat/corrupt (chromatica DSA).
    prof_npz=np.nansum(npz_model,0)
    target = data if (prof_npz.max()-prof_npz.min())/(abs(prof_npz.max())+1e-30) < 0.05 else npz_model
    M=np.einsum("nft,mft->fnm",Ks,Ks); b=np.einsum("nft,ft->fn",Ks,target)
    N=len(pscat); jit=1e-9*max(float(np.einsum("fnn->f",M).mean()),1e-30)
    g=np.linalg.solve(M+jit*np.eye(N),b[...,None])[...,0]
    mod_scat=np.einsum("fn,nft->ft",g,Ks); mod_intr=np.einsum("fn,nft->ft",g,Ki)
    prof_scat=np.nansum(mod_scat,0); prof_intr=np.nansum(mod_intr,0); prof_dat=np.nansum(data,0)
    scat_peak=t[0]+parpeak(prof_scat,int(np.argmax(prof_scat)))*dt
    data_peak=t[int(np.argmax(prof_dat))]
    arrival=first_moment(t,prof_intr)
    # localization err: rms of per-component t0 posterior half-widths
    if d.get("shared_zeta") or f"t0_{band}" in p:
        loc_err=perr.get(f"t0_{band}",0.0)
    else:
        es=[perr.get(f"t0_{band}{i}",0.0) for i in range(1,n+1)]
        loc_err=float(np.sqrt(np.mean(np.square(es))))
    return dict(burst=burstkey, band=tel, verdict_burst=vb, n_comp=n,
                tau_1ghz=float(tau), beta=float(beta), alpha=float(d["alpha"]["median"]),
                arrival_ms=float(arrival), scattered_peak_ms=float(scat_peak),
                data_peak_ms=float(data_peak), scatter_corr_ms=float(arrival-scat_peak),
                peak_reproduces_data_ms=float(scat_peak-data_peak),
                loc_err_ms=float(loc_err), dt_ms=dt, final=VMAP[vb]["final"])

# Prototype the decomposition-free scatter correction:
#   intrinsic_centroid_collapsed = scattered_centroid_collapsed - <PBF_mean>_fluxweighted
#   scatter_correction = intrinsic_centroid - scattered_peak
# PBF_mean(f) computed as centroid(K_scat[f]) - centroid(K_intr[f]) for a single
# representative unit kernel (gain/spectrum-independent). Flux weight from stored model.
import sys; sys.path.insert(0,"/tmp"); import numpy as np, json
from dataclasses import replace
import toa_recon2 as R2

def correction_v4(b, band):
    fit,npz,vb=R2.sources(b); d=json.load(open(fit)); p={k:v["median"] for k,v in d["percentiles"].items()}
    tel="chime" if band=="C" else "dsa"
    z=np.load(npz); t=np.asarray(z[f'time{band}'],float); freq=np.asarray(z[f'freq{band}'],float)
    npzm=np.asarray(z[f'model{band}'],float); noise=z[f'noise{band}']; dt=float(np.diff(t)[0])
    tau=float(z['tau_1ghz']); beta=float(z['beta'])
    m=R2.FRBModel(t,freq,data=npzm,dm_init=R2._cfg_dm_init(b,tel),df_MHz=R2.NATIVE_DF[tel],beta=beta,noise_std=noise)
    # representative single kernel at the (mean) t0/zeta of the fit (gain-independent centroid shift)
    pscat,_,_,n=R2._params(d,p,band,freq,tau_override=tau,beta_override=beta)
    # per-channel PBF mean = centroid(K_scat[f]) - centroid(K_intr[f]) averaged over components
    def chan_centroids(par):
        K=m(replace(par,c0=1.,gamma=0.),"M3")  # (F,T)
        w=np.clip(K,0,None); s=w.sum(1); s[s==0]=1
        return (K*t[None,:]).sum(1)/np.where(K.sum(1)==0,1,K.sum(1))
    # use flux-weighted average of per-component scatter delay
    dscat=np.zeros(len(freq)); wtot=0.0
    for par in pscat:
        cs=chan_centroids(replace(par,tau_1ghz=tau)); ci=chan_centroids(replace(par,tau_1ghz=0.0))
        dscat += (cs-ci); wtot+=1
    dscat/=wtot  # per-channel PBF mean (ms)
    # flux weight from stored model per-channel integrated flux
    flux=np.clip(np.nansum(npzm,1),0,None); flux[~np.isfinite(flux)]=0
    pbf_mean = float(np.sum(flux*dscat)/np.sum(flux)) if flux.sum()>0 else float(np.mean(dscat))
    prof=np.nansum(npzm,0)
    C_scat=R2.first_moment(t,prof)
    ip=int(np.argmax(prof)); P_scat=t[0]+R2.parpeak(prof,ip)*dt
    intrinsic_centroid = C_scat - pbf_mean
    corr = intrinsic_centroid - P_scat
    return dict(burst=b,band=tel,n=n,tau=tau,beta=beta,alpha=float(z['alpha']),
                C_scat=C_scat,P_scat=P_scat,data_peak=float(t[np.argmax(prof)]),
                pbf_mean=pbf_mean,intrinsic_centroid=intrinsic_centroid,scatter_corr=corr)

BURSTS=["casey","chromatica","freya","wilhelm","mahi","phineas","oran","isha","johndoeII","zach","hamilton","whitney"]
print(f"{'burst':12s}{'bd':4s}{'n':>2s}{'tau':>6s}{'C_scat':>9s}{'P_scat':>9s}{'pbf_mn':>8s}{'intr_cen':>9s}{'corr':>9s}")
for b in BURSTS:
    for band in ["C","D"]:
        r=correction_v4(b,band)
        print(f"{b:12s}{r['band'][:3]:4s}{r['n']:2d}{r['tau']:6.3f}{r['C_scat']:9.3f}{r['P_scat']:9.3f}{r['pbf_mean']:8.4f}{r['intrinsic_centroid']:9.3f}{r['scatter_corr']:+9.4f}")

python - <<'PYEOF'
# Parameter-direct intrinsic centroid: flux-weighted mean of component mu(f)=t0_c+dd(f),
# with component flux weights from NNLS onto the stored model (stable, non-negative).
import sys; sys.path.insert(0,"/tmp"); import numpy as np, json
from dataclasses import replace
from scipy.optimize import nnls
import toa_recon2 as R2

DM_DELAY=4.148808
def dd(freq, delta_dm, beta, fmax):
    return DM_DELAY*delta_dm*(freq**-beta - fmax**-beta)

def direct(b, band):
    fit,npz,vb=R2.sources(b); d=json.load(open(fit)); p={k:v["median"] for k,v in d["percentiles"].items()}
    tel="chime" if band=="C" else "dsa"
    z=np.load(npz); t=np.asarray(z[f'time{band}'],float); freq=np.asarray(z[f'freq{band}'],float)
    npzm=np.asarray(z[f'model{band}'],float); noise=z[f'noise{band}']; dt=float(np.diff(t)[0])
    tau=float(z['tau_1ghz']); beta=float(z['beta']); fmax=freq.max(); fcen=float(np.median(freq))
    m=R2.FRBModel(t,freq,data=npzm,dm_init=R2._cfg_dm_init(b,tel),df_MHz=R2.NATIVE_DF[tel],beta=beta,noise_std=noise)
    pscat,_,_,n=R2._params(d,p,band,freq,tau_override=tau,beta_override=beta)
    ddm=float(p.get(f"delta_dm_{band}",0.0))
    # component t0 list
    t0s=np.array([pp.t0 for pp in pscat])
    # NNLS per-channel gains onto stored model -> non-negative component fluxes
    Ks=np.stack([m(replace(pp,c0=1.,gamma=0.),"M3") for pp in pscat])  # (N,F,T)
    flux_c=np.zeros(n)
    for fi in range(len(freq)):
        A=Ks[:,fi,:].T  # (T,N)
        y=npzm[fi]
        try:
            g,_=nnls(A,y); flux_c+=g*np.array([Ks[j,fi].sum() for j in range(n)])
        except Exception:
            pass
    if flux_c.sum()<=0: flux_c=np.ones(n)
    w=flux_c/flux_c.sum()
    arrival_bandtop=float((w*t0s).sum())
    arrival_fcen = arrival_bandtop + dd(np.array([fcen]),ddm,beta,fmax)[0]
    prof=np.nansum(npzm,0); ip=int(np.argmax(prof)); P_scat=t[0]+R2.parpeak(prof,ip)*dt
    return dict(b=b,band=tel,n=n,t0s=list(np.round(t0s,3)),w=list(np.round(w,3)),
                arrival_bandtop=arrival_bandtop,arrival_fcen=float(arrival_fcen),
                P_scat=P_scat,corr=float(arrival_fcen-P_scat))

BURSTS=["casey","chromatica","freya","wilhelm","mahi","phineas","oran","isha","johndoeII","zach","hamilton","whitney"]
print(f"{'burst':12s}{'bd':4s}{'n':>2s}{'arr_top':>9s}{'arr_fcen':>9s}{'P_scat':>9s}{'corr':>9s}  t0s / weights")
for b in BURSTS:
    for band in ["C","D"]:
        r=direct(b,band)
        print(f"{b:12s}{r['band'][:3]:4s}{r['n']:2d}{r['arrival_bandtop']:9.3f}{r['arrival_fcen']:9.3f}{r['P_scat']:9.3f}{r['corr']:+9.4f}  {r['t0s']} {r['w']}")

import sys; sys.path.insert(0,"/tmp"); import numpy as np, json
from dataclasses import replace
from scipy.optimize import nnls
import toa_recon2 as R2

def band_v5(b, band):
    fit,npz,vb=R2.sources(b); d=json.load(open(fit)); p={k:v["median"] for k,v in d["percentiles"].items()}
    perr={k:(abs(v.get("err_minus",0.))+abs(v.get("err_plus",0.)))/2 for k,v in d["percentiles"].items()}
    tel="chime" if band=="C" else "dsa"
    z=np.load(npz); t=np.asarray(z[f'time{band}'],float); freq=np.asarray(z[f'freq{band}'],float)
    npzm=np.asarray(z[f'model{band}'],float); data=np.asarray(z[f'data{band}'],float)
    noise=z[f'noise{band}']; dt=float(np.diff(t)[0])
    tau=float(z['tau_1ghz']); beta=float(z['beta'])
    m=R2.FRBModel(t,freq,data=npzm,dm_init=R2._cfg_dm_init(b,tel),df_MHz=R2.NATIVE_DF[tel],beta=beta,noise_std=noise)
    pscat,_,_,n=R2._params(d,p,band,freq,tau_override=tau,beta_override=beta)
    pintr=[replace(pp,tau_1ghz=0.0) for pp in pscat]
    Ks=np.stack([m(replace(pp,c0=1.,gamma=0.),"M3") for pp in pscat])  # (N,F,T)
    Ki=np.stack([m(replace(pp,c0=1.,gamma=0.),"M3") for pp in pintr])
    prof_npz=np.nansum(npzm,0)
    target = data if (prof_npz.max()-prof_npz.min())/(abs(prof_npz.max())+1e-30)<0.05 else npzm
    # NNLS per-channel gains onto authoritative target (stable, non-negative)
    G=np.zeros((len(freq),n))
    for fi in range(len(freq)):
        A=Ks[:,fi,:].T; y=np.nan_to_num(target[fi])
        try: G[fi],_=nnls(A,y)
        except Exception: G[fi]=0.0
    mod_scat=np.einsum("fn,nft->ft",G,Ks); mod_intr=np.einsum("fn,nft->ft",G,Ki)
    resid=float(np.sqrt(np.nansum((mod_scat-target)**2)/(np.nansum(target**2)+1e-30)))
    ps=np.nansum(mod_scat,0); pI=np.nansum(mod_intr,0); pdat=np.nansum(data,0)
    scat_peak=t[0]+R2.parpeak(ps,int(np.argmax(ps)))*dt
    intr_cent=R2.first_moment(t,pI)
    intr_peak=t[0]+R2.parpeak(pI,int(np.argmax(pI)))*dt
    data_peak=float(t[int(np.argmax(pdat))])
    if d.get("shared_zeta") or f"t0_{band}" in p:
        loc=perr.get(f"t0_{band}",0.0)
    else:
        loc=float(np.sqrt(np.mean([perr.get(f"t0_{band}{i}",0.)**2 for i in range(1,n+1)])))
    return dict(burst=b,band=tel,n_comp=n,tau_1ghz=tau,beta=beta,alpha=float(z['alpha']),
                intrinsic_centroid_ms=intr_cent,intrinsic_peak_ms=intr_peak,
                scattered_peak_ms=scat_peak,data_peak_ms=data_peak,
                scatter_corr_ms=intr_cent-scat_peak,
                peak_repro_ms=scat_peak-data_peak,proj_resid=resid,
                loc_err_ms=loc,dt_ms=dt,final=R2.VMAP[vb]["final"])

BURSTS=["casey","chromatica","freya","wilhelm","mahi","phineas","oran","isha","johndoeII","zach","hamilton","whitney"]
print(f"{'burst':11s}{'bd':4s}{'n':>2s}{'i_cent':>8s}{'i_peak':>8s}{'s_peak':>8s}{'dat_pk':>8s}{'repro':>7s}{'resid':>6s}{'corr':>8s}")
rows=[]
for b in BURSTS:
    for band in ["C","D"]:
        r=band_v5(b,band); rows.append(r)
        print(f"{b:11s}{r['band'][:3]:4s}{r['n_comp']:2d}{r['intrinsic_centroid_ms']:8.3f}{r['intrinsic_peak_ms']:8.3f}{r['scattered_peak_ms']:8.3f}{r['data_peak_ms']:8.3f}{r['peak_repro_ms']:+7.3f}{r['proj_resid']:6.2f}{r['scatter_corr_ms']:+8.3f}")
json.dump(rows, open("/Users/jakobfaber/handoffs/toa_model/scatter_corrections_v5.json","w"), indent=2)

import sys; sys.path.insert(0,"/tmp"); import numpy as np, json
from dataclasses import replace
from scipy.optimize import nnls
import toa_recon2 as R2

def band_v6(b, band):
    fit,npz,vb=R2.sources(b); d=json.load(open(fit)); p={k:v["median"] for k,v in d["percentiles"].items()}
    perr={k:(abs(v.get("err_minus",0.))+abs(v.get("err_plus",0.)))/2 for k,v in d["percentiles"].items()}
    tel="chime" if band=="C" else "dsa"
    z=np.load(npz); t=np.asarray(z[f'time{band}'],float); freq=np.asarray(z[f'freq{band}'],float)
    npzm=np.asarray(z[f'model{band}'],float); data=np.asarray(z[f'data{band}'],float)
    noise=z[f'noise{band}']; dt=float(np.diff(t)[0])
    tau=float(z['tau_1ghz']); beta=float(z['beta'])
    m=R2.FRBModel(t,freq,data=npzm,dm_init=R2._cfg_dm_init(b,tel),df_MHz=R2.NATIVE_DF[tel],beta=beta,noise_std=noise)
    pscat,_,_,n=R2._params(d,p,band,freq,tau_override=tau,beta_override=beta)
    pintr=[replace(pp,tau_1ghz=0.0) for pp in pscat]
    Ks=np.stack([m(replace(pp,c0=1.,gamma=0.),"M3") for pp in pscat])
    Ki=np.stack([m(replace(pp,c0=1.,gamma=0.),"M3") for pp in pintr])
    prof_npz=np.nansum(npzm,0)
    target = data if (prof_npz.max()-prof_npz.min())/(abs(prof_npz.max())+1e-30)<0.05 else npzm
    G=np.zeros((len(freq),n))
    for fi in range(len(freq)):
        A=Ks[:,fi,:].T; y=np.nan_to_num(target[fi])
        try: G[fi],_=nnls(A,y)
        except Exception: G[fi]=0.0
    # per-component integrated scattered flux -> dominant component
    Fscat=np.array([ (G[:,j][:,None]*Ks[j]).sum() for j in range(n)])
    dom=int(np.argmax(Fscat))
    # per-component intrinsic centroid (clean Gaussians, stable), gain+flux weighted over freq
    def comp_intr_centroid(j):
        wf = G[:,j]*np.nansum(Ki[j],1)          # per-channel weight
        cf = np.array([R2.first_moment(t,Ki[j,fi]) for fi in range(len(freq))])
        s=wf.sum()
        return float((wf*cf).sum()/s) if s>0 else float(np.nan)
    intr_cent_dom=comp_intr_centroid(dom)
    intr_cent_all={j:comp_intr_centroid(j) for j in range(n)}
    # scattered collapsed peak (robust)
    mod_scat=np.einsum("fn,nft->ft",G,Ks); ps=np.nansum(mod_scat,0)
    scat_peak=t[0]+R2.parpeak(ps,int(np.argmax(ps)))*dt
    pdat=np.nansum(data,0); data_peak=float(t[int(np.argmax(pdat))])
    resid=float(np.sqrt(np.nansum((mod_scat-target)**2)/(np.nansum(target**2)+1e-30)))
    # localization error of the dominant component's t0
    if d.get("shared_zeta") or f"t0_{band}" in p:
        loc=perr.get(f"t0_{band}",0.0)
    else:
        loc=perr.get(f"t0_{band}{dom+1}", 0.0)
    return dict(burst=b,band=tel,n_comp=n,dominant=dom+1,tau_1ghz=tau,beta=beta,alpha=float(z['alpha']),
                intrinsic_centroid_ms=intr_cent_dom,scattered_peak_ms=scat_peak,data_peak_ms=data_peak,
                scatter_corr_ms=scat_peak-intr_cent_dom,  # Delta_scat = scat_peak - intrinsic (subtract from peakToA)
                peak_repro_ms=scat_peak-data_peak,proj_resid=resid,loc_err_ms=loc,
                comp_centroids={k:round(v,3) for k,v in intr_cent_all.items()},
                comp_flux=[round(float(x),1) for x in Fscat],dt_ms=dt,final=R2.VMAP[vb]["final"])

BURSTS=["casey","chromatica","freya","wilhelm","mahi","phineas","oran","isha","johndoeII","zach","hamilton","whitney"]
print(f"{'burst':11s}{'bd':4s}{'n':>2s}{'dom':>4s}{'i_cent':>8s}{'s_peak':>8s}{'dat_pk':>8s}{'repro':>7s}{'Δscat':>8s}  comps")
rows=[]
for b in BURSTS:
    for band in ["C","D"]:
        r=band_v6(b,band); rows.append(r)
        cc=r['comp_centroids']
        print(f"{b:11s}{r['band'][:3]:4s}{r['n_comp']:2d}{r['dominant']:4d}{r['intrinsic_centroid_ms']:8.3f}{r['scattered_peak_ms']:8.3f}{r['data_peak_ms']:8.3f}{r['peak_repro_ms']:+7.3f}{r['scatter_corr_ms']:+8.3f}  {cc if r['n_comp']>1 else ''}")

from dataclasses import replace
import toa_recon2 as R2

def band_v7(b, band):
    fit,npz,vb=R2.sources(b); d=json.load(open(fit)); p={k:v["median"] for k,v in d["percentiles"].items()}
    perr={k:(abs(v.get("err_minus",0.))+abs(v.get("err_plus",0.)))/2 for k,v in d["percentiles"].items()}
    tel="chime" if band=="C" else "dsa"
    z=np.load(npz); t=np.asarray(z[f'time{band}'],float); freq=np.asarray(z[f'freq{band}'],float)
    npzm=np.asarray(z[f'model{band}'],float); data=np.asarray(z[f'data{band}'],float)
    noise=z[f'noise{band}']; dt=float(np.diff(t)[0])
    tau=float(z['tau_1ghz']); beta=float(z['beta'])
    m=R2.FRBModel(t,freq,data=npzm,dm_init=R2._cfg_dm_init(b,tel),df_MHz=R2.NATIVE_DF[tel],beta=beta,noise_std=noise)
    pscat,_,_,n=R2._params(d,p,band,freq,tau_override=tau,beta_override=beta)
    pintr=[replace(pp,tau_1ghz=0.0) for pp in pscat]
    # per-channel PBF delay = flux-weighted over components of [peak(K_scat)-mu(K_intr)]
    Ks=np.stack([m(replace(pp,c0=1.,gamma=0.),"M3") for pp in pscat])   # (N,F,T)
    Ki=np.stack([m(replace(pp,c0=1.,gamma=0.),"M3") for pp in pintr])
    F=len(freq)
    def chan_peak(K1d):
        i=int(np.argmax(K1d)); return t[0]+R2.parpeak(K1d,i)*dt
    # per (comp,chan): scattered peak and intrinsic center
    dscat_fc=np.zeros((n,F)); wt_fc=np.zeros((n,F))
    for j in range(n):
        for fi in range(F):
            ks=Ks[j,fi]; ki=Ki[j,fi]
            fl=ki.sum()
            if fl<=1e-3*Ki[j].max()*len(t)+1e-30 and Ki[j].max()>0:  # channel dispersed off-axis
                wt_fc[j,fi]=0.0; continue
            dscat_fc[j,fi]=chan_peak(ks)-chan_peak(ki)
            wt_fc[j,fi]=fl
    # component flux (scattered) for weighting components
    Fcomp=np.array([ (Ks[j]).sum() for j in range(n)])  # unit-gain relative flux ~ equal; use model channel flux weighting instead
    # collapse weights: model per-channel flux, and equal component weight (unit gain)
    modflux=np.clip(np.nansum(npzm,1),0,None)
    W = wt_fc * modflux[None,:]
    if W.sum()<=0: W=wt_fc+1e-9
    Delta_scat = float(np.sum(W*dscat_fc)/np.sum(W))
    # scattered collapsed peak & data peak (for reproduction check) using NNLS gains
    from scipy.optimize import nnls
    prof_npz=np.nansum(npzm,0); target=data if (prof_npz.max()-prof_npz.min())/(abs(prof_npz.max())+1e-30)<0.05 else npzm
    G=np.zeros((F,n))
    for fi in range(F):
        A=Ks[:,fi,:].T; y=np.nan_to_num(target[fi])
        try: G[fi],_=__import__('scipy.optimize',fromlist=['nnls']).nnls(A,y)
        except Exception: G[fi]=0
    mod_scat=np.einsum("fn,nft->ft",G,Ks); ps=np.nansum(mod_scat,0)
    scat_peak=t[0]+R2.parpeak(ps,int(np.argmax(ps)))*dt
    data_peak=float(t[int(np.argmax(np.nansum(data,0)))])
    resid=float(np.sqrt(np.nansum((mod_scat-target)**2)/(np.nansum(target**2)+1e-30)))
    # localization: dominant comp t0 error
    dom=int(np.argmax([ (G[:,j][:,None]*Ks[j]).sum() for j in range(n)]))
    if d.get("shared_zeta") or f"t0_{band}" in p: loc=perr.get(f"t0_{band}",0.0)
    else: loc=perr.get(f"t0_{band}{dom+1}",0.0)
    return dict(burst=b,band=tel,n_comp=n,tau_1ghz=tau,beta=beta,alpha=float(z['alpha']),
                Delta_scat_ms=Delta_scat, scattered_peak_ms=scat_peak, data_peak_ms=data_peak,
                peak_repro_ms=scat_peak-data_peak, proj_resid=resid, loc_err_ms=loc,
                dt_ms=dt, final=R2.VMAP[vb]["final"])

BURSTS=["casey","chromatica","freya","wilhelm","mahi","phineas","oran","isha","johndoeII","zach","hamilton","whitney"]
print(f"{'burst':11s}{'bd':4s}{'n':>2s}{'tau':>6s}{'beta':>6s}{'Δscat':>8s}{'s_peak':>8s}{'dat_pk':>8s}{'repro':>7s}{'resid':>6s}{'locerr':>8s}")
rows=[]
for b in BURSTS:
    for band in ["C","D"]:
        r=band_v7(b,band); rows.append(r)
        print(f"{b:11s}{r['band'][:3]:4s}{r['n_comp']:2d}{r['tau_1ghz']:6.3f}{r['beta']:6.2f}{r['Delta_scat_ms']:+8.4f}{r['scattered_peak_ms']:8.3f}{r['data_peak_ms']:8.3f}{r['peak_repro_ms']:+7.3f}{r['proj_resid']:6.2f}{r['loc_err_ms']:8.4f}")
json.dump(rows, open("/Users/jakobfaber/handoffs/toa_model/scatter_corrections_v7.json","w"), indent=2)
print("\nDifferential (CHIME-DSA) scatter shift per burst:")
for i in range(0,len(rows),2):
    C=rows[i]; D=rows[i+1]
    print(f"  {C['burst']:11s} diff = {C['Delta_scat_ms']-D['Delta_scat_ms']:+.4f} ms")

            mu = p.t0 + dd_full[freq_subset, None]

        # Intra-channel smearing scales with the TRUE DM offset from the de-smear
        # baseline: dm_init (the DM the data was dedispersed at) plus the fitted
        # residual delta_dm. With dm_init=0 in the configs, omitting delta_dm here
        # left a fitted residual DM (e.g. CHIME delta_dm~4.7) with zero modelled
        # smearing, so its real smearing leaked into zeta/tau and biased alpha
        # (Codex review, 2026-06). hypot() squares sig_dm, so a negative residual
        # gives the same |ΔDM| smearing magnitude.
        dm_smear = self.dm_init + p.delta_dm
        if model_key in {"M1", "M3"}:
            sig_full = self._smearing_sigma(dm_smear, p.zeta)
        else:
            sig_full = self._smearing_sigma(dm_smear, 0.0)

        if freq_subset is None:
            sig = sig_full[:, None]
        else:
            sig = sig_full[freq_subset, None]

        # Guard against non-physical width
        sig = np.clip(sig, 1e-6, None)

        if model_key in {"M2", "M3"} and p.tau_1ghz > 1e-6:
            alpha = p.alpha
            tau = p.tau_1ghz * (freq / 1.0) ** (-alpha)
            tau = np.clip(tau, 1e-6, None)[:, None]

            beta_pbf = float(np.clip(p.beta, BETA_THIN_SCREEN_MIN, BETA_THIN_SCREEN_MAX))
            if beta_pbf >= BETA_THIN_SCREEN_MAX - BETA_EXP_EPS:
                return amp[:, None] * analytic_gaussian_exp_convolution(self.time, mu, sig, tau)
            return amp[:, None] * gaussian_powerlaw_convolution(
                self.time, mu, sig, tau, beta_pbf
            )

        # Non-scattering models (M0, M1) or negligible tau
        gauss = (1.0 / (np.sqrt(2.0 * np.pi) * sig)) * np.exp(-0.5 * ((self.time - mu) / sig) ** 2)
        # Normalize
        gauss_sum = np.sum(gauss, axis=1, keepdims=True)
        safe_gauss_sum = np.clip(gauss_sum, 1e-30, None)

        if model_key not in {"M0", "M1", "M2", "M3"}:
            raise ValueError(f"unknown model '{model_key}'")

        return amp[:, None] * (gauss / safe_gauss_sum)

    def log_likelihood(self, p: FRBParams, model: str = "M3") -> float:
        if self.data is None or self.noise_std is None:
            raise RuntimeError("need observed data + noise_std for likelihood")

        # Use pre-calculated validity mask
        if self.valid is None or not np.any(self.valid):
            return -np.inf

        # Broadcast noise to full shape before masking
        ns = self.noise_std
        if ns.ndim == 1:
            ns = ns[:, None]
        noise_std_full = np.broadcast_to(ns, self.data.shape)
        noise_valid = noise_std_full[self.valid]
        data_valid = self.data[self.valid]

+"""Robust model-ToA scatter correction (v8).
+
+Uses ONLY the authoritative stored NPZ scattered model + the analytic PBF mean.
+  Delta_scat = P_scat - C_scat + <PBF_mean>_fluxweighted   [subtract from peak-ToA]
+  intrinsic_centroid_collapsed = C_scat - <PBF_mean>
+where P_scat/C_scat are the collapsed peak/centroid of the NPZ model (robust,
+no reconstruction), and <PBF_mean>(f) = centroid(unit scattered kernel) -
+centroid(unit intrinsic kernel), a pure property of tau(f),beta (gain/component
+independent since convolution shifts the centroid by the PBF mean regardless of
+the intrinsic shape).
+"""
+from __future__ import annotations
+import json, os
+from dataclasses import replace
+import numpy as np, yaml
+
+REPO="/Users/jakobfaber/Developer/repos/github.com/jakobtfaber/dsa110-FLITS"
+import sys; sys.path.insert(0,REPO); sys.path.insert(0,f"{REPO}/scattering")
+from scat_analysis.burstfit import FRBParams, FRBModel  # noqa
+import toa_recon2 as R2   # reuse sources(), _params(), VMAP, NATIVE_DF, _cfg_dm_init, parpeak, first_moment
+
+def _pbf_mean_per_channel(m, par_scat, t, freq):
+    """Per-channel PBF mean delay = centroid(scattered unit kernel) - centroid(intrinsic)."""
+    Ks=m(replace(par_scat,c0=1.,gamma=0.),"M3")               # (F,T) scattered
+    Ki=m(replace(par_scat,c0=1.,gamma=0.,tau_1ghz=0.0),"M3")  # (F,T) intrinsic
+    out=np.full(len(freq),np.nan)
+    for fi in range(len(freq)):
+        ws=np.clip(Ks[fi],0,None); wi=np.clip(Ki[fi],0,None)
+        ss=ws.sum(); si=wi.sum()
+        if ss>0 and si>0:
+            out[fi]=(t*ws).sum()/ss - (t*wi).sum()/si
+    return out
+
+def band_result(b, band):
+    fit,npz,vb=R2.sources(b); d=json.load(open(fit)); p={k:v["median"] for k,v in d["percentiles"].items()}
+    perr={k:(abs(v.get("err_minus",0.))+abs(v.get("err_plus",0.)))/2 for k,v in d["percentiles"].items()}
+    tel="chime" if band=="C" else "dsa"
+    z=np.load(npz); t=np.asarray(z[f"time{band}"],float); freq=np.asarray(z[f"freq{band}"],float)
+    npzm=np.asarray(z[f"model{band}"],float); data=np.asarray(z[f"data{band}"],float)
+    noise=z[f"noise{band}"]; dt=float(np.diff(t)[0])
+    tau=float(z["tau_1ghz"]); beta=float(z["beta"])
+    # authoritative collapsed scattered profile; fall back to data if model flat/corrupt
+    prof_m=np.nansum(npzm,0)
+    use_model = (prof_m.max()-prof_m.min())/(abs(prof_m.max())+1e-30) >= 0.05
+    src = npzm if use_model else data
+    prof=np.nansum(src,0)
+    C_scat=R2.first_moment(t,prof)
+    P_scat=t[0]+R2.parpeak(prof,int(np.argmax(prof)))*dt
+    data_peak=float(t[int(np.argmax(np.nansum(data,0)))])
+    # analytic PBF mean, flux-weighted by authoritative per-channel flux
+    m=FRBModel(t,freq,data=src,dm_init=R2._cfg_dm_init(b,tel),df_MHz=R2.NATIVE_DF[tel],beta=beta,noise_std=noise)
+    pscat,_,_,n=R2._params(d,p,band,freq,tau_override=tau,beta_override=beta)
+    pbf=_pbf_mean_per_channel(m,pscat[0],t,freq)          # PBF mean same for all comps
+    chflux=np.clip(np.nansum(src,1),0,None)
+    ok=np.isfinite(pbf) & (chflux>0)
+    pbf_mean=float(np.sum(chflux[ok]*pbf[ok])/np.sum(chflux[ok])) if ok.any() else float(np.nanmean(pbf))
+    intr_centroid=C_scat-pbf_mean
+    Delta_scat=P_scat-intr_centroid          # = P_scat - C_scat + pbf_mean
+    # localization error: dominant-component t0 posterior half-width
+    if d.get("shared_zeta") or f"t0_{band}" in p:
+        loc=perr.get(f"t0_{band}",0.0)
+    else:
+        t0s={i:p.get(f"t0_{band}{i}") for i in range(1,n+1) if p.get(f"t0_{band}{i}") is not None}
+        dom=min(t0s,key=lambda i:abs(t0s[i]-P_scat)) if t0s else 1
+        loc=perr.get(f"t0_{band}{dom}",0.0)
+    return dict(burst=b,band=tel,verdict_burst=vb,n_comp=n,tau_1ghz=tau,beta=beta,alpha=float(z["alpha"]),
+                collapsed_centroid_ms=C_scat, collapsed_peak_ms=P_scat, data_peak_ms=data_peak,
+                pbf_mean_ms=pbf_mean, intrinsic_centroid_ms=intr_centroid,
+                scatter_corr_ms=Delta_scat, loc_err_ms=float(loc),
+                used_model=bool(use_model), dt_ms=dt, final=R2.VMAP[vb]["final"])
+
+if __name__=="__main__":
+    import sys as s; print(band_result(s.argv[1],s.argv[2]))
+

import sys; sys.path.insert(0,"/tmp"); import numpy as np, json
from dataclasses import replace
import toa_recon2 as R2
from scat_analysis.burstfit import analytic_gaussian_exp_convolution, gaussian_powerlaw_convolution

# Peak shift of G(0,sig) (x) PBF(tau,beta), computed on a DENSE zero-centered grid.
# Immune to data-axis truncation, dispersion sweep, gains. Function of (sig,tau,beta) only.
def peak_shift(sig, tau, beta, dt_fine=None):
    span=max(20*tau, 12*sig, 2.0)
    dt=min(sig,tau)/50.0 if dt_fine is None else dt_fine
    dt=max(dt, span/200000)  # cap grid size
    tg=np.arange(-span, span, dt)
    if beta>=3.98:
        y=analytic_gaussian_exp_convolution(tg, 0.0, sig, tau)
    else:
        y=gaussian_powerlaw_convolution(tg, 0.0, sig, tau, beta)
    i=int(np.argmax(y))
    # parabolic refine
    if 0<i<len(y)-1:
        d=y[i-1]-2*y[i]+y[i+1]
        off=0.5*(y[i-1]-y[i+1])/d if d!=0 else 0.0
    else: off=0.0
    return (tg[i]+off*dt)   # peak position relative to Gaussian center (0)

def band_v9(b, band):
    fit,npz,vb=R2.sources(b); d=json.load(open(fit)); p={k:v["median"] for k,v in d["percentiles"].items()}
    tel="chime" if band=="C" else "dsa"
    z=np.load(npz); t=np.asarray(z[f'time{band}'],float); freq=np.asarray(z[f'freq{band}'],float)
    npzm=np.asarray(z[f'model{band}'],float); data=np.asarray(z[f'data{band}'],float)
    noise=z[f'noise{band}']; dt=float(np.diff(t)[0])
    tau=float(z['tau_1ghz']); beta=float(z['beta'])
    m=R2.FRBModel(t,freq,data=npzm,dm_init=R2._cfg_dm_init(b,tel),df_MHz=R2.NATIVE_DF[tel],beta=beta,noise_std=noise)
    pscat,_,_,n=R2._params(d,p,band,freq,tau_override=tau,beta_override=beta)
    # dominant component: nearest t0 to collapsed peak
    prof_npz=np.nansum(npzm,0); use_model=(prof_npz.max()-prof_npz.min())/(abs(prof_npz.max())+1e-30)>=0.05
    src=npzm if use_model else data; prof=np.nansum(src,0)
    P_scat=t[0]+R2.parpeak(prof,int(np.argmax(prof)))*dt
    t0s={i:pscat[i-1].t0 for i in range(1,n+1)}; dom=min(t0s,key=lambda i:abs(t0s[i]-P_scat))
    par=pscat[dom-1]
    # per-channel sig (smearing) and tau from the model internals
    alpha=par.alpha
    tau_f=np.clip(tau*(freq/1.0)**(-alpha),1e-6,None)
    sig_f=m._smearing_sigma(m.dm_init+par.delta_dm, par.zeta)  # (F,)
    sig_f=np.clip(np.atleast_1d(sig_f),1e-6,None)
    if sig_f.size==1: sig_f=np.full(len(freq),float(sig_f))
    # per-channel peak shift, flux-weighted by authoritative model channel flux
    chflux=np.clip(np.nansum(src,1),0,None)
    ok=chflux>0
    ps=np.array([peak_shift(sig_f[fi],tau_f[fi],beta) for fi in range(len(freq))])
    Dscat=float(np.sum(chflux[ok]*ps[ok])/np.sum(chflux[ok]))
    return dict(burst=b,band=tel,n=n,dom=dom,tau=tau,beta=beta,alpha=float(alpha),
                Delta_scat=Dscat, P_scat=P_scat, data_peak=float(t[np.argmax(np.nansum(data,0))]),
                tau_cen=float(np.sum(chflux[ok]*tau_f[ok])/np.sum(chflux[ok])))

BURSTS=["casey","chromatica","freya","wilhelm","mahi","phineas","oran","isha","johndoeII","zach","hamilton","whitney"]
print(f"{'burst':11s}{'bd':4s}{'n':>2s}{'dom':>4s}{'tau1':>6s}{'beta':>6s}{'tau_cen':>8s}{'Δscat_pk':>9s}{'P_scat':>8s}{'dat_pk':>8s}")
rows=[]
for b in BURSTS:
    for band in ["C","D"]:
        r=band_v9(b,band); rows.append(r)
        print(f"{b:11s}{r['band'][:3]:4s}{r['n']:2d}{r['dom']:4d}{r['tau']:6.3f}{r['beta']:6.2f}{r['tau_cen']:8.3f}{r['Delta_scat']:+9.4f}{r['P_scat']:8.3f}{r['data_peak']:8.3f}")
print("\nchromatica benchmark: Phase-0 BurstDataset gave Delta_scat_C(peak-vs-centroid)=+0.361, (peak-vs-peak)=+0.436")
json.dump(rows, open("/Users/jakobfaber/handoffs/toa_model/scatter_corrections_v9.json","w"), indent=2)

# direct check: peak of G(mu=10,sig)(x)exp(tau) minus 10 should equal dense-grid peak_shift
def peak_shift_dense(sig,tau,beta=4.0):
    span=max(20*tau,12*sig,2.0); dt=min(sig,tau)/50.0; dt=max(dt,span/200000)
    tg=np.arange(-span,span,dt); y=analytic_gaussian_exp_convolution(tg,0.0,sig,tau)
    i=int(np.argmax(y)); d=y[i-1]-2*y[i]+y[i+1]; off=0.5*(y[i-1]-y[i+1])/d if d else 0

+"""Final model-ToA scatter correction (v10).
+
+The bias in the current peak-based ToA is the *peak shift* of the intrinsic
+Gaussian under the scattering PBF, averaged over the band with the burst's own
+spectrum. For each channel we compute the peak position of a unit
+Gaussian(sig_f) convolved with the PBF(tau_f, beta) on a DENSE zero-centred grid
+(the peak shift is a pure function of sig_f, tau_f, beta -- gain-, dispersion-,
+and truncation-immune), then flux-weight over channels using the authoritative
+stored model's per-channel flux.
+
+  Delta_scat(band) = < peak_shift(sig_f, tau_f, beta) >_fluxweighted   [ms, >=0]
+
+This is the amount to SUBTRACT from the peak-based ToA to recover the intrinsic
+(pre-scattering) arrival. sig_f and tau_f are read from the model internals for
+the dominant component (nearest t0 to the collapsed peak). Validated: the
+dense-grid peak position reproduces a direct on-axis convolution peak to 1e-4 ms.
+"""
+from __future__ import annotations
+import json
+from dataclasses import replace
+import numpy as np
+
+REPO="/Users/jakobfaber/Developer/repos/github.com/jakobtfaber/dsa110-FLITS"
+import sys; sys.path.insert(0,REPO); sys.path.insert(0,f"{REPO}/scattering")
+from scat_analysis.burstfit import (
+    FRBModel, analytic_gaussian_exp_convolution as _agec,
+    gaussian_powerlaw_convolution as _gpc, BETA_THIN_SCREEN_MIN,
+    BETA_THIN_SCREEN_MAX, BETA_EXP_EPS,
+)
+import toa_recon2 as R2
+
+def peak_shift(sig, tau, beta):
+    """Peak position of G(0,sig) (x) PBF(tau,beta) on a dense grid. Array-shaped calls."""
+    sig=float(max(sig,1e-6)); tau=float(max(tau,1e-6))
+    span=max(20*tau, 12*sig, 2.0); dt=min(sig,tau)/50.0; dt=max(dt, span/400000)
+    tg=np.arange(-span, span, dt)
+    mu=np.array([[0.0]]); S=np.array([[sig]]); T=np.array([[tau]])
+    bpbf=float(np.clip(beta, BETA_THIN_SCREEN_MIN, BETA_THIN_SCREEN_MAX))
+    if bpbf >= BETA_THIN_SCREEN_MAX - BETA_EXP_EPS:
+        y=_agec(tg, mu, S, T)[0]
+    else:
+        y=_gpc(tg, mu, S, T, bpbf)[0]
+    i=int(np.argmax(y))
+    if 0<i<len(y)-1:
+        d=y[i-1]-2*y[i]+y[i+1]; off=0.5*(y[i-1]-y[i+1])/d if d!=0 else 0.0
+    else: off=0.0
+    return float(tg[i]+off*dt)
+
+def band_result(b, band):
+    fit,npz,vb=R2.sources(b); d=json.load(open(fit)); p={k:v["median"] for k,v in d["percentiles"].items()}
+    perr={k:(abs(v.get("err_minus",0.))+abs(v.get("err_plus",0.)))/2 for k,v in d["percentiles"].items()}
+    tel="chime" if band=="C" else "dsa"
+    z=np.load(npz); t=np.asarray(z[f"time{band}"],float); freq=np.asarray(z[f"freq{band}"],float)
+    npzm=np.asarray(z[f"model{band}"],float); data=np.asarray(z[f"data{band}"],float)
+    noise=z[f"noise{band}"]; dt=float(np.diff(t)[0])
+    tau=float(z["tau_1ghz"]); beta=float(z["beta"])
+    m=FRBModel(t,freq,data=npzm,dm_init=R2._cfg_dm_init(b,tel),df_MHz=R2.NATIVE_DF[tel],beta=beta,noise_std=noise)
+    pscat,_,_,n=R2._params(d,p,band,freq,tau_override=tau,beta_override=beta)
+    # collapsed peak (authoritative model, fall back to data if flat)
+    prof_m=np.nansum(npzm,0); use_model=(prof_m.max()-prof_m.min())/(abs(prof_m.max())+1e-30)>=0.05
+    src=npzm if use_model else data; prof=np.nansum(src,0)
+    P_scat=t[0]+R2.parpeak(prof,int(np.argmax(prof)))*dt
+    data_peak=float(t[int(np.argmax(np.nansum(data,0)))])
+    # dominant component: t0 nearest the collapsed peak
+    t0s={i:pscat[i-1].t0 for i in range(1,n+1)}
+    dom=min(t0s,key=lambda i:abs(t0s[i]-P_scat)); par=pscat[dom-1]
+    alpha=par.alpha
+    tau_f=np.clip(tau*(freq/1.0)**(-alpha),1e-6,None)
+    sig_f=np.atleast_1d(m._smearing_sigma(m.dm_init+par.delta_dm, par.zeta)).astype(float)
+    if sig_f.size==1: sig_f=np.full(len(freq),float(sig_f))
+    sig_f=np.clip(sig_f,1e-6,None)
+    chflux=np.clip(np.nansum(src,1),0,None); ok=chflux>0
+    ps=np.array([peak_shift(sig_f[fi],tau_f[fi],beta) for fi in range(len(freq))])
+    Delta_scat=float(np.sum(chflux[ok]*ps[ok])/np.sum(chflux[ok]))
+    # per-channel tau/sig band-center summaries
+    tau_cen=float(np.sum(chflux[ok]*tau_f[ok])/np.sum(chflux[ok]))
+    # localization error: dominant-component t0 posterior half-width
+    if d.get("shared_zeta") or f"t0_{band}" in p:
+        loc=perr.get(f"t0_{band}",0.0)
+    else:
+        loc=perr.get(f"t0_{band}{dom}",0.0)
+    intrinsic_toa=P_scat-Delta_scat
+    return dict(burst=b,band=tel,verdict_burst=vb,n_comp=n,dominant_comp=dom,
+                tau_1ghz=tau,beta=beta,alpha=float(alpha),tau_center_ms=tau_cen,
+                collapsed_peak_ms=P_scat,data_peak_ms=data_peak,
+                scatter_corr_ms=Delta_scat,intrinsic_toa_ms=intrinsic_toa,
+                loc_err_ms=float(loc),used_model=bool(use_model),dt_ms=dt,
+                final=R2.VMAP[vb]["final"])
+
+if __name__=="__main__":
+    import sys as s; print(band_result(s.argv[1],s.argv[2]))
+

        tau_center_C_ms=round(C["tau_center_ms"],4), tau_center_D_ms=round(D["tau_center_ms"],4),

        # existing peak-based offset and the model-corrected offset
        peak_offset_ms=round(peak_off,4) if peak_off is not None else None,
        model_corrected_offset_ms=round(corr_off,4) if corr_off is not None else None,
        used_stored_model_C=C["used_model"], used_stored_model_D=D["used_model"],
    ))
df=pd.DataFrame(rows).sort_values("burst").reset_index(drop=True)
df.to_csv("/Users/jakobfaber/handoffs/toa_model/toa_scatter_correction_table.csv", index=False)
pd.set_option("display.width",240,"display.max_columns",40)

print("\nrows:",len(df),"  cols:",list(df.columns))

import sys; sys.path.insert(0,"/tmp"); import importlib, json
import numpy as np, pandas as pd
import toa_recon_final as RF; importlib.reload(RF)

xm=json.load(open("crossmatching/toa_crossmatch_results.json"))
BURSTS=["casey","chromatica","freya","wilhelm","mahi","phineas","oran","isha","johndoeII","zach","hamilton","whitney"]
rows=[]
for b in BURSTS:
    C=RF.band_result(b,"C"); D=RF.band_result(b,"D"); x=xm.get(b,{})
    peak_off=x.get("measured_offset_ms")
    diff=C["scatter_corr_ms"]-D["scatter_corr_ms"]            # Delta_scat_C - Delta_scat_D  (>0)
    # true arrival = peak - Delta_scat on each band -> corrected offset = peak_off - differential
    corr_off = (peak_off - diff) if peak_off is not None else None
    # localization error added in quadrature to the existing DM-referral error
    ec=x.get("error_chime_ms"); ed=x.get("error_dsa_ms")
    loc_C=C["loc_err_ms"]; loc_D=D["loc_err_ms"]
    err_chime_new = float(np.hypot(ec, loc_C)) if ec is not None else None
    err_dsa_new   = float(np.hypot(ed, loc_D)) if ed is not None else None
    comb_new = float(np.hypot(err_chime_new, err_dsa_new)) if (err_chime_new is not None and err_dsa_new is not None) else None
    rows.append(dict(
        burst=b, verdict=C["final"], n_comp_C=C["n_comp"], n_comp_D=D["n_comp"],
        tau_1ghz_ms=round(C["tau_1ghz"],4), beta=round(C["beta"],3), alpha=round(C["alpha"],3),
        tau_center_C_ms=round(C["tau_center_ms"],4), tau_center_D_ms=round(D["tau_center_ms"],4),
        chime_peak_toa_axis_ms=round(C["collapsed_peak_ms"],4),
        dsa_peak_toa_axis_ms=round(D["collapsed_peak_ms"],4),
        chime_data_peak_ms=round(C["data_peak_ms"],4),
        dsa_data_peak_ms=round(D["data_peak_ms"],4),
        chime_model_toa_axis_ms=round(C["intrinsic_toa_ms"],4),
        dsa_model_toa_axis_ms=round(D["intrinsic_toa_ms"],4),
        scatter_corr_C_ms=round(C["scatter_corr_ms"],4),
        scatter_corr_D_ms=round(D["scatter_corr_ms"],4),
        differential_scatter_shift_ms=round(diff,4),
        loc_err_C_ms=round(loc_C,4), loc_err_D_ms=round(loc_D,4),
        peak_offset_ms=round(peak_off,4) if peak_off is not None else None,
        model_corrected_offset_ms=round(corr_off,4) if corr_off is not None else None,
        offset_shift_ms=round(corr_off-peak_off,4) if corr_off is not None else None,
        error_chime_peak_ms=round(ec,5) if ec is not None else None,
        error_dsa_peak_ms=round(ed,5) if ed is not None else None,
        error_chime_model_ms=round(err_chime_new,5) if err_chime_new is not None else None,
        error_dsa_model_ms=round(err_dsa_new,5) if err_dsa_new is not None else None,
        combined_err_peak_ms=round(x.get("combined_dm_uncertainty_ms"),5) if x.get("combined_dm_uncertainty_ms") is not None else None,
        combined_err_model_ms=round(comb_new,5) if comb_new is not None else None,
        used_stored_model_C=C["used_model"], used_stored_model_D=D["used_model"],
    ))
df=pd.DataFrame(rows).sort_values("burst").reset_index(drop=True)
df.to_csv("/Users/jakobfaber/handoffs/toa_model/toa_scatter_correction_table.csv", index=False)
pd.set_option("display.width",260,"display.max_columns",50)
print(df[["burst","tau_1ghz_ms","scatter_corr_C_ms","scatter_corr_D_ms","differential_scatter_shift_ms","peak_offset_ms","model_corrected_offset_ms","offset_shift_ms"]].to_string(index=False))
print("\n=== sanity: differential > 0 always (CHIME scattered more)? ===")
print("  all diff>0:", bool((df["differential_scatter_shift_ms"]>0).all()))
print("  offset_shift == -differential (within rounding)?", bool(np.allclose(df["offset_shift_ms"], -df["differential_scatter_shift_ms"], atol=1e-3)))

# skill:figure-style kernel.py (auto-injected on skill load)
META_GREY = "#888888"


def apply_figure_style(*, frame="open", font=None, sizes=(8, 7, 6), grid=False):
    """Set matplotlib rcParams for publication-grade output. Call once before plotting.

    This sets mechanics (role-mapped font-size ladder, outward ticks, frameless
    legends, 300-dpi save, Type-42 embedded fonts) — not a house aesthetic.
    Frame, font and the size ladder are parameters.

    frame : 'open' (bottom+left spines, default) | 'boxed' (all four) | 'none'
    font  : sans-serif family name; None = system default sans-serif
    sizes : (base, secondary, tick) — titles/axis-labels, legend/annotation, ticks
    grid  : whether to draw axes.grid (default False)
    """
    import matplotlib as mpl
    if frame not in ("open", "boxed", "none"):
        raise ValueError(f"frame must be 'open'|'boxed'|'none', got {frame!r}")

    try:
        import os, sys, glob, matplotlib.font_manager as fm
        fdir = os.path.join(os.environ.get("CONDA_PREFIX") or sys.prefix, "fonts")
        if os.path.isdir(fdir):
            known = {f.fname for f in fm.fontManager.ttflist}
            for f in glob.glob(os.path.join(fdir, "*.ttf")):
                if f not in known:
                    fm.fontManager.addfont(f)
    except Exception:
        pass
    base, secondary, tick = sizes
    boxed = (frame == "boxed")
    rc = {
        "font.family": "sans-serif",
        "font.size": base,
        "axes.labelsize": base,
        "axes.titlesize": base,
        "legend.fontsize": secondary,
        "xtick.labelsize": tick,
        "ytick.labelsize": tick,
        "axes.linewidth": 0.6,
        "xtick.direction": "out", "ytick.direction": "out",
        "xtick.major.size": 3, "ytick.major.size": 3,
        "xtick.major.width": 0.6, "ytick.major.width": 0.6,
        "axes.spines.top": boxed, "axes.spines.right": boxed,
        "axes.spines.left": frame != "none", "axes.spines.bottom": frame != "none",
        "axes.grid": bool(grid),
        "legend.frameon": False,
        "figure.dpi": 200,
        "savefig.dpi": 300,
        "savefig.bbox": "tight",
        "axes.titleweight": "normal",
        "axes.titlelocation": "left",
        "axes.labelweight": "normal",
        "lines.linewidth": 1.2,
        "patch.linewidth": 0.6,
        "pdf.fonttype": 42, "ps.fonttype": 42,
    }
    if font:
        rc["font.sans-serif"] = [font, "DejaVu Sans"]
    mpl.rcParams.update(rc)


def set_frame(ax, style="open"):
    """§3: set spine visibility on an existing axes. style ∈ {'open','boxed','none'}."""
    show = {"open": (False, False, True, True),
            "boxed": (True, True, True, True),
            "none": (False, False, False, False)}[style]
    for side, vis in zip(("top", "right", "bottom", "left"), show):
        ax.spines[side].set_visible(vis)
        if vis:
            ax.spines[side].set_linewidth(0.6)
    ax.tick_params(direction="out", length=0 if style == "none" else 3, width=0.6)


def panel_letter(ax, letter, dx=-0.18, dy=1.02, case="lower", fontsize=None):
    """§5.7: bold panel letter outside top-left of axes. case ∈ {'lower','upper'}."""
    import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
    if fontsize is None:
        fontsize = plt.rcParams.get("font.size", 8) + 1
    s = letter.lower() if case == "lower" else letter.upper()
    ax.text(dx, dy, s, transform=ax.transAxes,
            fontweight="bold", fontsize=fontsize, va="bottom", ha="left")


def focal_palette(labels, focal, focal_color, other="muted", base_colors=None):
    """§4.2: map labels → colors with the focal series visually dominant.

    other='muted'   — desaturate base_colors (or a default cycle) toward gray
    other='grey'    — uniform light gray for all non-focal
    other='ordinal' — non-focal on a single light→dark gray ramp (input order)
    """
    import matplotlib.colors as mcolors
    import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
    focal_set = {focal} if isinstance(focal, str) else set(focal)
    n = len(labels)
    if not focal_set & set(labels):
        raise ValueError(f"focal {focal!r} not found in labels")
    if base_colors is None:
        base_colors = plt.rcParams["axes.prop_cycle"].by_key().get("color", ["#444444"])
    base_colors = [base_colors[i % len(base_colors)] for i in range(n)]
    if other == "grey":
        rest = ["#BCBCBC"] * n
    elif other == "ordinal":
        nf = max(1, n - len(focal_set))
        ramp = [mcolors.to_hex((v, v, v)) for v in
                ([0.55] if nf == 1 else [0.80 - 0.35 * i / (nf - 1) for i in range(nf)])]
        rest, k = [], 0
        for l in labels:
            rest.append(ramp[min(k, nf - 1)]); k += (l not in focal_set)
    else:
        def mute(c):
            r, g, b = mcolors.to_rgb(c)
            m = (r + g + b) / 3
            return mcolors.to_hex((0.3 * r + 0.7 * m, 0.3 * g + 0.7 * m, 0.3 * b + 0.7 * m))
        rest = [mute(c) for c in base_colors]
    return [focal_color if l in focal_set else rest[i] for i, l in enumerate(labels)]


def bar_with_points(ax, x, ymat, labels, colors, jitter=0.08, show_points=True,
                    errorbar=None, point_alpha=0.5, point_size=8):
    """§6.1: bar = mean; optionally overlay raw points or draw an interval.

    colors   : per-label color list (e.g. from focal_palette)
    errorbar : None | 'sd' | 'ci95' — drawn only when show_points is False.
               'ci95' is the t-distribution 95% CI of the mean
               (half-width t_{0.975,n-1} · s/√n); correct at small n where the
               z-approximation (1.96·s/√n) is markedly too narrow.
    """
    import numpy as np
    means = np.array([np.mean(y) for y in ymat], float)
    err = None
    if errorbar and not show_points:
        if errorbar == "sd":
            err = np.array([np.std(y, ddof=1) if np.asarray(y).size > 1 else 0 for y in ymat])
        elif errorbar == "ci95":
            from scipy.stats import t
            def _hw(y):
                n = np.asarray(y).size
                return t.ppf(0.975, n - 1) * np.std(y, ddof=1) / np.sqrt(n) if n > 1 else 0
            err = np.array([_hw(y) for y in ymat])
    ax.bar(x, means, color=colors, width=0.7, edgecolor="none",
           yerr=err, error_kw={"elinewidth": 0.8, "capsize": 0})
    if show_points:
        for xi, ys in zip(x, ymat):
            ys = np.asarray(ys)
            if ys.ndim and ys.size > 1:
                jit = (np.random.rand(ys.size) - 0.5) * 2 * jitter
                ax.scatter(np.full(ys.size, xi) + jit, ys, s=point_size, color="black",
                           alpha=point_alpha, zorder=3, linewidths=0)
    ax.set_xticks(x); ax.set_xticklabels(labels)
    return ax


def strip_with_median(ax, groups, values, colors=None, jitter=0.12):
    """§6.1: jittered points + bold horizontal median tick per group."""
    import numpy as np
    labs = list(groups)
    if colors is None:
        colors = ["#444444"] * len(labs)
    for i, (ys, c) in enumerate(zip(values, colors)):
        ys = np.asarray(ys)
        jit = (np.random.rand(ys.size) - 0.5) * 2 * jitter
        ax.scatter(np.full(ys.size, i) + jit, ys, s=10, color=c, alpha=0.6, linewidths=0, zorder=2)
        m = np.median(ys)
        ax.plot([i - 0.22, i + 0.22], [m, m], color="black", lw=1.6, zorder=3)
    ax.set_xticks(range(len(labs))); ax.set_xticklabels(labs)
    return ax


def goodness_arrow(ax, text="higher = better", loc="upper left", axis="y", fontsize=None):
    """§3.6: small upright direction-of-goodness cue in the margin."""
    import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
    if fontsize is None:
        fontsize = plt.rcParams["legend.fontsize"]
    pos = {"upper left": (0.02, 0.98), "upper right": (0.98, 0.98),
           "lower left": (0.02, 0.02), "lower right": (0.98, 0.02)}[loc]
    ha = "left" if "left" in loc else "right"
    va = "top" if "upper" in loc else "bottom"
    arrow = "↑ " if axis == "y" else "→ "
    ax.text(pos[0], pos[1], arrow + text, transform=ax.transAxes,
            fontsize=fontsize, color=META_GREY, ha=ha, va=va)


def two_tier_label(name, meta):
    """§5: two-line label string (name / metadata). Meta line styled separately by caller."""
    return f"{name}\n{meta}"


def end_of_line_labels(ax, xs, ys, labels, colors=None, dx=0.01, fontsize=None):
    """§6.3 / §7.3: label each line series at its right end instead of a legend box."""
    import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
    if fontsize is None:
        fontsize = plt.rcParams["font.size"]
    if colors is None:
        colors = [None] * len(labels)
    span = ax.get_xlim()[1] - ax.get_xlim()[0]
    for x, y, lab, c in zip(xs, ys, labels, colors):
        ax.text(x[-1] + dx * span, y[-1], lab, color=c, va="center", ha="left", fontsize=fontsize)


def panel_crops(fig, dpi=None, pad_px=6, bbox_inches=None, pad_inches=None):
    """§9.2: pixel-space crop boxes for each lettered panel in the SAVED PNG.

    Returns ``{letter: (x0, y0, x1, y1)}`` in image-space pixels (origin
    top-left, matching ``host.view_image(path, crop=...)`` and PIL's
    ``Image.crop``). Panels are detected as bold single-character ``Text``
    objects placed by :func:`panel_letter`; each panel's crop is its axes'
    tightbbox mapped into the saved file's pixel space, padded by ``pad_px``.
    For §3.4 composites (abutting subplots sharing an axis, letter on the
    leftmost only) the crop unions in letterless ``sharex``/``sharey`` siblings
    on the same grid row/col so the whole composite is covered. When no axes
    carries a panel letter (standalone plot, or a figure-composer sub-agent),
    falls back to one crop per axes keyed by index.

    ``bbox_inches`` mirrors ``Figure.savefig`` semantics: ``None`` means
    *consult rcParams* (so under :func:`apply_figure_style` it resolves to
    ``'tight'``); pass an explicit ``Bbox`` only if you saved with one. The
    boxes are clamped to the saved image extent regardless.

        >>> fig.savefig("fig.png")            # bbox_inches='tight' via rcParams
        >>> for letter, box in panel_crops(fig).items():
        ...     host.view_image("fig.png", crop=box)
    """
    import matplotlib as mpl
    import matplotlib.text
    if dpi is None:
        dpi = mpl.rcParams.get("savefig.dpi", fig.dpi)
        if dpi == "figure":
            dpi = fig.dpi
    dpi = float(dpi)
    if bbox_inches is None:
        bbox_inches = mpl.rcParams.get("savefig.bbox")
    fig.canvas.draw()
    r = fig.canvas.get_renderer()

    if bbox_inches == "tight":
        if pad_inches is None:
            pad_inches = mpl.rcParams.get("savefig.pad_inches", 0.1)
        tb = fig.get_tightbbox(r).padded(pad_inches)
        ox_in, oy_in = tb.x0, tb.y0
        W_in, H_in = tb.width, tb.height
    elif isinstance(bbox_inches, mpl.transforms.BboxBase):
        ox_in, oy_in = bbox_inches.x0, bbox_inches.y0
        W_in, H_in = bbox_inches.width, bbox_inches.height
    else:
        ox_in, oy_in = 0.0, 0.0
        W_in, H_in = fig.get_size_inches()
    W_px, H_px = int(round(W_in * dpi)), int(round(H_in * dpi))
    lettered = {}
    for ax in fig.axes:
        for t in ax.findobj(matplotlib.text.Text):
            s = (t.get_text() or "").strip()
            if len(s) == 1 and s.isalpha() and t.get_fontweight() in ("bold", 700):
                lettered[ax] = s
                break



    if not lettered:
        lettered = {ax: str(i) for i, ax in enumerate(fig.axes)}
    out = {}
    for ax, letter in lettered.items():
        bbs = [ax.get_tightbbox(r)]




        ss = ax.get_subplotspec()
        for sib in fig.axes:
            if sib is ax or sib in lettered:
                continue
            ssib = sib.get_subplotspec()
            same_row = ss is None or ssib is None or ss.rowspan == ssib.rowspan
            same_col = ss is None or ssib is None or ss.colspan == ssib.colspan
            if ((ax.get_shared_y_axes().joined(ax, sib) and same_row)
                    or (ax.get_shared_x_axes().joined(ax, sib) and same_col)):
                bbs.append(sib.get_tightbbox(r))
        bb = mpl.transforms.Bbox.union(bbs)

        bx0 = (bb.x0 / fig.dpi - ox_in) * dpi
        bx1 = (bb.x1 / fig.dpi - ox_in) * dpi
        by0 = H_px - (bb.y1 / fig.dpi - oy_in) * dpi
        by1 = H_px - (bb.y0 / fig.dpi - oy_in) * dpi
        out[letter] = (
            max(int(bx0) - pad_px, 0),
            max(int(by0) - pad_px, 0),
            min(int(bx1) + pad_px, W_px),
            min(int(by1) + pad_px, H_px),
        )
    return out

import matplotlib as mpl, matplotlib.pyplot as plt

d = pd.read_csv("toa_scatter_correction_table.csv")

y = np.arange(len(d))

fig, (axL, axR) = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(9.2, 5.0), gridspec_kw={"width_ratios":[1.35,1.0], "wspace":0.32})

# ---- Panel A: peak vs model-corrected offset, per burst ----
c_peak, c_model = focal_palette(2)[1], focal_palette(2)[0]   # model = focal
for yi, r in d.iterrows():
    axL.plot([r.peak_offset_ms, r.model_corrected_offset_ms], [yi, yi],
             color=META_GREY, lw=1.0, zorder=1)
axL.scatter(d.peak_offset_ms, y, s=34, color=c_peak, zorder=3, label="peak-bin ToA")
axL.scatter(d.model_corrected_offset_ms, y, s=40, color=c_model, marker="D", zorder=3, label="scatter-corrected model ToA")
axL.axvline(0, color="0.7", lw=0.8, ls=":", zorder=0)
axL.set_yticks(y); axL.set_yticklabels(d.burst)
axL.set_xlabel("CHIME \u2212 DSA offset at 400 MHz (ms)")
axL.set_title("Scatter correction moves every offset earlier", loc="left")
axL.margins(y=0.04)
axL.legend(frameon=False, loc="lower right", fontsize=6)
# annotate the extreme (oran)
ro = d[d.burst=="oran"].iloc[0]; yo = d.index[d.burst=="oran"][0]
axL.annotate(f"\u0394 = {ro.differential_scatter_shift_ms:+.2f} ms",
             xy=((ro.peak_offset_ms+ro.model_corrected_offset_ms)/2, yo),
             xytext=(0, 9), textcoords="offset points", ha="center", fontsize=6, color=META_GREY)

# ---- Panel B: differential scatter shift vs tau ----
axR.scatter(d.tau_1ghz_ms, d.differential_scatter_shift_ms, s=38, color=c_model, zorder=3)
for _, r in d.iterrows():
    if r.differential_scatter_shift_ms > 0.3 or r.tau_1ghz_ms > 1.0:
        axR.annotate(r.burst, (r.tau_1ghz_ms, r.differential_scatter_shift_ms),
                     xytext=(4,2), textcoords="offset points", fontsize=6, color=META_GREY)
axR.set_xlabel(r"$\tau_{1\,\mathrm{GHz}}$ (ms)")
axR.set_ylabel(r"differential scatter shift $\Delta_C-\Delta_D$ (ms)")
axR.set_title("Shift grows with scattering", loc="left")
axR.margins(0.06)

fig.savefig("toa_peak_vs_model_comparison.png", dpi=300, bbox_inches="tight")
fig.savefig("toa_peak_vs_model_comparison.pdf", bbox_inches="tight")

print("text overlaps:", len(ov))

=== the reproduction test structure ===
import json
from pathlib import Path

import pytest

from crossmatching.toa_crossmatch import (
    DsaTimingProvenance,
    crossmatch_input_from_dict,
    reproduce_notebook_result,
)

ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
FIXTURE = ROOT / "crossmatching" / "notebook_reproduction_fixture.json"
GOLDEN = ROOT / "crossmatching" / "toa_crossmatch_results.json"


def _load_json(path):
    return json.loads(path.read_text())


def test_reproduces_notebook_crossmatch_results():
    fixture = _load_json(FIXTURE)
    golden = _load_json(GOLDEN)

    assert len(fixture["bursts"]) == len(golden) == 12

    for row in fixture["bursts"]:
        expected = golden[row["name"]]
        result = reproduce_notebook_result(crossmatch_input_from_dict(row)).to_legacy_dict()

        for key in ("chime_id", "dm", "dm_mjd", "fwhm_ms"):
            assert result[key] == expected[key]

        assert result["toa_dsa_utc_400"] == expected["toa_dsa_utc_400"]
        assert result["combined_dm_uncertainty_ms"] == pytest.approx(
            expected["combined_dm_uncertainty_ms"], abs=1e-12
        )
        assert result["measured_offset_ms"] == pytest.approx(
            expected["measured_offset_ms"], abs=2e-3
        )
        assert result["geometric_delay_ms"] == pytest.approx(
            expected["geometric_delay_ms"], abs=1e-2
        )


def test_dsa_filterbank_header_is_provenance_not_curated_time():
    dsa = DsaTimingProvenance(
        dsa_mjd=60369.37095224303,
        filterbank_tstart_mjd=60369.37095,
        tsamp_s=3.2768e-05,
        nchans=6144,
        fch1_mhz=1498.75,
        foff_mhz=-0.03051757812,
    )

    assert dsa.curated_time.mjd == pytest.approx(60369.37095224303)
    assert dsa.filterbank_tstart_mjd == pytest.approx(60369.37095)
    assert abs((dsa.curated_time.mjd - dsa.filterbank_tstart_mjd) * 86400) > dsa.tsamp_s

=== toa_crossmatch.py head (imports + provenance classes, 1-58) ===
from __future__ import annotations

import logging
from dataclasses import asdict, dataclass
from typing import Any

import astropy.constants as const
import astropy.units as u
import numpy as np
from astropy.coordinates import EarthLocation, SkyCoord
from astropy.time import Time

from flits.common.constants import K_DM
from flits.common.utils import calculate_dm_timing_error

# Assume these are defined elsewhere in your script
# from baseband_analysis.core.bbdata import BBData
# from baseband_analysis.core.dedispersion import delay_across_the_band


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ChimeTimingProvenance:
    """Notebook-derived CHIME timing facts used for reproduction."""

    toa_utc_400: str
    toa_unix_400: float | None = None
    baseband_path: str | None = None
    baseband_vospace_uri: str | None = None
    baseband_verified_exists: bool | None = None
    baseband_vls_listing: str | None = None
    peak_index: int | None = None
    delta_time_s: float | None = None
    center_frequency_mhz: float | None = None

    @property
    def toa_time_400(self) -> Time:
        if self.toa_unix_400 is not None:
            return Time(self.toa_unix_400, format="unix", scale="utc")
        return Time(self.toa_utc_400, format="iso", scale="utc")


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class DsaTimingProvenance:
    """Curated DSA timing plus filterbank facts kept as provenance."""

    dsa_mjd: float
    native_frequency_mhz: float = 1530.0
    reference_frequency_mhz: float = 400.0
    filterbank_path: str | None = None
    filterbank_tstart_mjd: float | None = None
    tsamp_s: float | None = None
    nchans: int | None = None
    fch1_mhz: float | None = None
    foff_mhz: float | None = None

    @property
    def curated_time(self) -> Time:
        return Time(self.dsa_mjd, format="mjd", scale="utc")

-def compute_toa(t0, offset, f_center, DM, f_ref):
-    """Compute a time of arrival referenced to a frequency.
-
-    Parameters
-    ----------
-    t0 : astropy.units.Quantity or astropy.time.Time
-        Reference time. If a Quantity, assumed to be seconds since the Unix
-        epoch.
-    offset : astropy.units.Quantity
-        Instrumental or processing offset to add.
-    f_center : astropy.units.Quantity
-        Central observing frequency in MHz.
-    DM : astropy.units.Quantity
-        Dispersion measure.
-    f_ref : astropy.units.Quantity
-        Reference frequency in MHz.
-
-    Returns
-    -------
-    astropy.time.Time
-        Time of arrival referred to ``f_ref``.
-    """
-    shift = K_DM * DM.value * (1 / f_ref.value**2 - 1 / f_center.value**2) * u.s
-    if isinstance(t0, Time):
-        return t0 + offset + shift
-    toa = t0 + offset + shift
-    return Time(toa.to_value(u.s), format="unix", scale="utc")
+def compute_toa(t0, offset, f_center, DM, f_ref, scatter_correction=0.0 * u.s):
+    """Compute a time of arrival referenced to a frequency.
+
+    Parameters
+    ----------
+    t0 : astropy.units.Quantity or astropy.time.Time
+        Reference time. If a Quantity, assumed to be seconds since the Unix
+        epoch.
+    offset : astropy.units.Quantity
+        Instrumental or processing offset to add.
+    f_center : astropy.units.Quantity
+        Central observing frequency in MHz.
+    DM : astropy.units.Quantity
+        Dispersion measure.
+    f_ref : astropy.units.Quantity
+        Reference frequency in MHz.
+    scatter_correction : astropy.units.Quantity, optional
+        Scattering peak-shift for this band (>= 0). The peak of a scattered
+        profile lags the intrinsic arrival by this amount, so it is
+        *subtracted* to recover the model (de-scattered) time of arrival. The
+        default of zero reproduces the legacy peak-based ToA. See
+        :func:`reproduce_model_result` and the joint-fit scatter-correction
+        table for how the per-band value is derived.
+
+    Returns
+    -------
+    astropy.time.Time
+        Time of arrival referred to ``f_ref``.
+    """
+    shift = K_DM * DM.value * (1 / f_ref.value**2 - 1 / f_center.value**2) * u.s
+    if isinstance(t0, Time):
+        return t0 + offset + shift - scatter_correction
+    toa = t0 + offset + shift - scatter_correction
+    return Time(toa.to_value(u.s), format="unix", scale="utc")

+    # the model error budget in quadrature with the DM-referral term.

-def crossmatch_input_from_dict(row: dict[str, Any]) -> CrossmatchInput:
+        measured_offset_ms=measured_offset_ms,
+        combined_dm_uncertainty_ms=combined_dm_uncertainty_ms,
+        geometric_delay_ms=geometric_delay_ms,
+    )

+def reproduce_model_result(crossmatch: CrossmatchInput) -> CrossmatchResult:
+    """Model (scatter-corrected) crossmatch result.
+
+    Identical to :func:`reproduce_notebook_result` except that the per-band
+    joint-fit scattering peak-shift is subtracted from each band's ToA, so the
+    reported ``measured_offset_ms`` is the intrinsic (de-scattered) CHIME-DSA
+    offset at 400 MHz. The legacy peak-based offset is retained as
+    ``peak_measured_offset_ms``. The error budget folds the posterior
+    localization error into the DM-referral term in quadrature per band.
+
+    Requires ``scatter_corr_chime_ms``/``scatter_corr_dsa_ms`` on the input; if
+    absent it falls back to the legacy (zero-correction) result so callers can
+    use it uniformly.
+    """
+    if (
+        crossmatch.scatter_corr_chime_ms is None
+        or crossmatch.scatter_corr_dsa_ms is None
+    ):
+        return reproduce_notebook_result(crossmatch)
+
+    dm = crossmatch.dm * (u.pc / u.cm**3)
+    scat_chime = crossmatch.scatter_corr_chime_ms * u.ms
+    scat_dsa = crossmatch.scatter_corr_dsa_ms * u.ms
+
+    # CHIME ToA is stored provenance already referred to 400 MHz; subtract its
+    # scattering peak-shift directly to recover the intrinsic arrival.
+    chime_toa = crossmatch.chime.toa_time_400 - scat_chime
+    dsa_toa = compute_toa(
+        crossmatch.dsa.curated_time,
+        0.0 * u.s,
+        crossmatch.dsa.native_frequency_mhz * u.MHz,
+        dm,
+        crossmatch.dsa.reference_frequency_mhz * u.MHz,
+        scatter_correction=scat_dsa,
+    )
+    measured_offset_ms = (chime_toa - dsa_toa).to_value(u.ms)
+    peak_measured_offset_ms = reproduce_notebook_result(crossmatch).measured_offset_ms
+    differential_scatter_shift_ms = float(
+        crossmatch.scatter_corr_chime_ms - crossmatch.scatter_corr_dsa_ms
+    )
+
+    src = SkyCoord(crossmatch.source_coord, unit=(u.hourangle, u.deg), frame="icrs")
+    geometric_delay_ms = compute_geometric_delay(
+        chime_toa,
+        src,
+        EarthLocation.of_site("DRAO"),
+        EarthLocation.of_site("OVRO"),
+    ).to_value(u.ms)
+
+    # Error budget: fold posterior localization into the DM-referral term.
+    err_chime = crossmatch.error_chime_ms
+    err_dsa = crossmatch.error_dsa_ms
+    loc_chime = crossmatch.loc_err_chime_ms
+    loc_dsa = crossmatch.loc_err_dsa_ms
+    err_chime_model = (
+        float(np.hypot(err_chime, loc_chime))
+        if err_chime is not None and loc_chime is not None
+        else err_chime
+    )
+    err_dsa_model = (
+        float(np.hypot(err_dsa, loc_dsa))
+        if err_dsa is not None and loc_dsa is not None
+        else err_dsa
+    )
+    combined_error_ms = None
+    if err_chime_model is not None and err_dsa_model is not None:
+        combined_error_ms = float(np.hypot(err_chime_model, err_dsa_model))
+    combined_dm_uncertainty_ms = None
+    if err_chime is not None and err_dsa is not None:
+        combined_dm_uncertainty_ms = float(np.hypot(err_chime, err_dsa))
+
+    return CrossmatchResult(
+        chime_id=crossmatch.chime_id,
+        dm=crossmatch.dm,
+        fwhm_ms=crossmatch.fwhm_ms,
+        toa_chime_unix_400=crossmatch.chime.toa_unix_400,
+        toa_chime_utc_400=chime_toa.iso,
+        dm_mjd=crossmatch.dsa.dsa_mjd,
+        toa_dsa_utc_400=dsa_toa.iso,
+        dm_uncertainty=crossmatch.dm_uncertainty,
+        error_chime_ms=err_chime_model,
+        error_dsa_ms=err_dsa_model,
+        measured_offset_ms=measured_offset_ms,
+        combined_dm_uncertainty_ms=combined_dm_uncertainty_ms,
+        geometric_delay_ms=geometric_delay_ms,
+        scatter_corr_chime_ms=crossmatch.scatter_corr_chime_ms,
+        scatter_corr_dsa_ms=crossmatch.scatter_corr_dsa_ms,
+        differential_scatter_shift_ms=differential_scatter_shift_ms,
+        peak_measured_offset_ms=peak_measured_offset_ms,
+        loc_err_chime_ms=loc_chime,
+        loc_err_dsa_ms=loc_dsa,
+        combined_error_ms=combined_error_ms,
+    )
+
+
+def crossmatch_input_from_dict(row: dict[str, Any]) -> CrossmatchInput:

import urllib.request, json
# try https for the sites registry
for url in ["https://data.astropy.org/coordinates/sites.json"]:
    try:
        req=urllib.request.Request(url, headers={})
        data=urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=20).read()
        j=json.loads(data)
        print("OK", url, "len", len(data), "n_sites", len(j))
        open("/tmp/sites.json","wb").write(data)

+from flits.common.constants import K_DM
+from flits.common.utils import calculate_dm_timing_error
+
+# Observatory locations for the CHIME (DRAO) and DSA-110 (OVRO) sites. These
+# are held as explicit literals rather than EarthLocation.of_site(...) so the
+# crossmatch and its tests are deterministic and do not depend on astropy's
+# remote site registry (data.astropy.org). The values match the canonical
+# coordinates used throughout the pipeline; a site-position uncertainty of even
+# 1 km projects to < 3e-3 ms of geometric delay, far below any reported
+# precision. of_site("DRAO")/of_site("OVRO") agree with these to within metres.
+DRAO_LOCATION = EarthLocation(lat=49.3206 * u.deg, lon=-119.6236 * u.deg, height=545 * u.m)
+OVRO_LOCATION = EarthLocation(lat=37.2333 * u.deg, lon=-118.2834 * u.deg, height=1222 * u.m)

+def test_reproduces_notebook_crossmatch_results():
+    """Legacy peak-based path.
+
+    The golden ``measured_offset_ms`` is now the model (scatter-corrected)
+    offset, so the legacy peak offset is asserted against
+    ``peak_measured_offset_ms``. ``combined_dm_uncertainty_ms`` remains the pure
+    DM-referral term and is unchanged by the model switch.
+    """
+    fixture = _load_json(FIXTURE)
+    golden = _load_json(GOLDEN)
+
+    assert len(fixture["bursts"]) == len(golden) == 12
+
+    for row in fixture["bursts"]:
+        expected = golden[row["name"]]
+        result = reproduce_notebook_result(crossmatch_input_from_dict(row)).to_legacy_dict()
+
+        for key in ("chime_id", "dm", "dm_mjd", "fwhm_ms"):
+            assert result[key] == expected[key]
+
+        assert result["toa_dsa_utc_400"] == expected["toa_dsa_utc_400"]
+        assert result["combined_dm_uncertainty_ms"] == pytest.approx(
+            expected["combined_dm_uncertainty_ms"], abs=1e-12
+        )
+        # Legacy peak offset -> golden peak_measured_offset_ms.
+        assert result["measured_offset_ms"] == pytest.approx(
+            expected["peak_measured_offset_ms"], abs=2e-3
+        )
+        assert result["geometric_delay_ms"] == pytest.approx(
+            expected["geometric_delay_ms"], abs=1e-2
+        )
+
+
+def test_reproduces_model_crossmatch_results():
+    """Model (scatter-corrected) path reproduces the golden model offsets.
+
+    Asserts the intrinsic offset, the per-band scatter corrections, the
+    differential shift, and the folded error budget. The model ToA subtracts
+    each band's joint-fit scattering peak-shift, so ``measured_offset_ms`` =
+    peak offset - (Delta_scat_C - Delta_scat_D).
+    """
+    fixture = _load_json(FIXTURE)
+    golden = _load_json(GOLDEN)
+
+    for row in fixture["bursts"]:
+        expected = golden[row["name"]]
+        result = reproduce_model_result(crossmatch_input_from_dict(row)).to_model_dict()
+
+        # Model offset and its provenance.
+        assert result["measured_offset_ms"] == pytest.approx(
+            expected["measured_offset_ms"], abs=2e-3
+        )
+        assert result["peak_measured_offset_ms"] == pytest.approx(
+            expected["peak_measured_offset_ms"], abs=2e-3
+        )
+        for key in (
+            "scatter_corr_chime_ms",
+            "scatter_corr_dsa_ms",
+            "differential_scatter_shift_ms",
+            "loc_err_chime_ms",
+            "loc_err_dsa_ms",
+        ):
+            assert result[key] == pytest.approx(expected[key], abs=1e-9)
+
+        # Offset relation: model = peak - differential.
+        assert result["measured_offset_ms"] == pytest.approx(
+            result["peak_measured_offset_ms"]
+            - result["differential_scatter_shift_ms"],
+            abs=2e-3,
+        )
+
+        # Folded error budget: combined = hypot(chime_model, dsa_model).
+        assert result["combined_error_ms"] == pytest.approx(
+            (result["error_chime_ms"] ** 2 + result["error_dsa_ms"] ** 2) ** 0.5,
+            abs=1e-9,
+        )
+        # Scattering peak-shift is non-negative and CHIME >= DSA.
+        assert result["scatter_corr_chime_ms"] >= 0.0
+        assert result["scatter_corr_dsa_ms"] >= 0.0
+        assert result["differential_scatter_shift_ms"] >= -1e-9
+
+
+def test_legacy_dict_schema_is_unchanged():
+    """to_legacy_dict must remain the original 13-key row (no model leakage)."""
+    fixture = _load_json(FIXTURE)
+    row = fixture["bursts"][0]
+    legacy = reproduce_notebook_result(crossmatch_input_from_dict(row)).to_legacy_dict()
+    assert set(legacy.keys()) == {
+        "chime_id", "dm", "fwhm_ms", "toa_chime_unix_400", "toa_chime_utc_400",
+        "dm_mjd", "toa_dsa_utc_400", "dm_uncertainty", "error_chime_ms",
+        "error_dsa_ms", "measured_offset_ms", "combined_dm_uncertainty_ms",
+        "geometric_delay_ms",
+    }

-        return None
-    return float(row["measured_offset_ms"])
+def toa_offset_ms(burst: str, toa_json: Path | None = None) -> float | None:
+    """Offset used to align the two bands' *observed* cube peaks for display.
+
+    This is the peak-based CHIME-DSA offset, not the scatter-corrected model
+    offset: the codetection gallery overlays the observed (scattered) profiles,
+    so their peaks must be aligned. Since the model switch, the golden JSON's
+    ``measured_offset_ms`` is the intrinsic (de-scattered) offset and the peak
+    offset lives in ``peak_measured_offset_ms``. Prefer the peak key; fall back
+    to ``measured_offset_ms`` for older JSONs that predate the model extension.
+    """
+    fp = toa_json or _TOA_JSON
+    if not fp.exists():
+        return None
+    rows = json.loads(fp.read_text())
+    row = rows.get(burst) or rows.get(burst.lower())
+    if not row:
+        return None
+    offset = row.get("peak_measured_offset_ms", row.get("measured_offset_ms"))
+    return float(offset) if offset is not None else None

stdout:
\subsubsection{Residuals and systematics}

Table~\ref{tab:sample} summarizes the association diagnostics. The tabulated
residuals use the shared DSA-DM convention: the CHIME and DSA arrivals are both
referenced to 400\,MHz with the DSA catalog DM, so the residual is a
timing/geometry consistency check rather than an independent per-telescope-DM
comparison. Re-referencing the CHIME arrivals with their independently measured
DMs would inject millisecond-scale shifts for the largest CHIME--DSA DM offsets,
so those shifts are tracked separately rather than folded into the association
residual.

We adopt the sign convention $\Delta t = \Delta t_{\rm inter\text{-}site} -
\tau_{\rm geo}$, where $\Delta t_{\rm inter\text{-}site}$ is the CHIME-minus-DSA
arrival offset after both are referenced to 400\,MHz (Eq.~\ref{eq:dmdelay}) and
$\tau_{\rm geo}$ is the predicted geometric delay (Eq.~\ref{eq:geodelay}); a
positive residual means the CHIME arrival is later than the shared-DM geometric
prediction relative to DSA, and $\Delta t=0$ is exact agreement. The geometric
term is itself bounded: for the $1345\,\mathrm{km}$ CHIME (DRAO)--DSA-110 (OVRO)
baseline, $|\tau_{\rm geo}| \le |\mathbf{p}_2-\mathbf{p}_1|/c \approx 4.5\,$ms for
any source direction. For the twelve source directions here,
$\tau_{\rm geo}=-2.30$ to $-2.03\,$ms: the wavefront reaches CHIME first and
DSA-110 $2.03$--$2.30\,$ms later. The residual is therefore not error-free but carries a
per-burst systematic of order the inter-site clock/timestamp alignment
($\sigma_{\rm clock}\sim1$\,ms) plus, for the eight originally DM-conditioned
pairs, the residual dispersive delay left by the CHIME--DSA DM offset. With the
new phase-coherence measurements, the largest band offset is
$0.117\,\mathrm{pc\,cm^{-3}}$ (FRB~20221113A), corresponding to approximately
$2.8$\,ms when re-referenced between 1.5\,GHz and 400\,MHz,
a convention-dependent term reported separately rather than folded in. We
assign each pair a $1\sigma$ timing budget $\sigma_i$ by combining, in
quadrature, the shared-DM combined CHIME+DSA arrival error, the intrinsic pulse
width, and the $\sigma_{\rm clock}\sim1$\,ms term, and accept a pair when its
residual is consistent with the geometric delay to within $|\Delta t_i| \le
3\sigma_i$. The budget is dominated by the pulse width for the two broadest
bursts, so the largest raw residual---$+8.4$\,ms for FRB~20220506D, a $74$\,ms-wide
burst---is only $0.1\sigma$. All twelve residuals ($-2.7$ to $+8.4$\,ms,
tabulated with their $\sigma_i$ in Table~\ref{tab:sample}) satisfy the criterion;
the tightest is FRB~20221203A at $2.6\sigma$. A residual of several tens of ms
for a narrow, well-localized burst---inconsistent with both its few-ms budget and
the $\lesssim\!4.5$\,ms geometric delay---would fail.
Because nine of the twelve residuals are positive, we also test for a
constant inter-site timing offset: the inverse-variance-weighted mean
residual is $+2.1\pm0.9$\,ms ($2.4\sigma$ from zero), a network-level shift
comparable to the $\sigma_{\rm clock}\sim1$\,ms per-site timestamp systematic
already carried in the budget, and a constant-offset model is only mildly
preferred over zero offset ($\chi^{2}=8.9/11$ versus $14.7/12$). Subtracting
the fitted offset leaves every pair within $1.8\sigma$ of the geometric
prediction (maximum $2.6\sigma$ without subtraction), so the acceptance
verdicts are unchanged in either convention; we retain the unsubtracted
residuals in Table~\ref{tab:sample} and flag the weighted mean as a candidate
inter-site calibration term for the archival release. We stress
that the association verdict does not rest on the timing residual alone: the
decisive gates are the chance-coincidence probability, positional coincidence,
and (for the eight bursts with a pre-specified DM-conditioned association
term) DM agreement, for which the residual is a
supporting consistency diagnostic.

Evaluated with the conservative windows above, the eight originally
DM-conditioned bursts
have $P_{\rm cc}<10^{-8}$ individually, while each of the four conservatively
classified pairs has the deliberately less selective position-and-time-only
value $P_{\rm cc}=4.4\times10^{-7}$. Thus all twelve have $P_{\rm cc}<10^{-6}$,
and their sample-summed expectation is $\sum_i\mu_i\approx1.8\times10^{-6}$.
Because these class-aware estimates remain far below unity, the
chance-coincidence test establishes the co-detections; positional coincidence
and the independently measured phase-coherence DMs provide additional checks.
All twelve pass the positional-coincidence check and now have constrained
CHIME and DSA phase-coherence measurements (Table~\ref{tab:dm-measurements}).
The four pairs marked `assoc.~(pos.)' in Table~\ref{tab:sample} retain that
label because their pre-specified association calculation used position and
timing alone; we do not apply a post-selection DM factor. For those four we set
$f_{\rm DM}=1$ in
Eq.~\eqref{eq:pcc_mu}---the DM criterion is not imposed, so their
$P_{\rm cc}$ is a time-and-position bound only, consistent with the
position-only verdict; the eight originally DM-conditioned bursts carry the full
$f_{\rm DM}(\mathrm{DM}_i,\Delta\mathrm{DM})<1$ factor. The reported
$P_{\rm cc}$ values are thus computed per association class, and the
position-only $P_{\rm cc}$ are, if anything, conservative relative to the
originally DM-conditioned ones.

The per-burst DM provenance (per-telescope DM, method, and CHIME--DSA agreement)
is given in Table~\ref{tab:dm-measurements} and in the accompanying
machine-readable catalog; the intermediate association diagnostics are
provided in the archival data release (Section~\ref{sec:data-availability});
Table~\ref{tab:sample} carries the manuscript-facing timing residual, $P_{\rm cc}$,
and association-verdict columns. Across the twelve phase-coherence fits,
$\mathrm{DM_{CHIME}}-\mathrm{DM_{DSA}}$ has mean
$+0.013\,\mathrm{pc\,cm^{-3}}$ and maximum absolute value
$0.117\,\mathrm{pc\,cm^{-3}}$; no coherent $\sim0.4\,\mathrm{pc\,cm^{-3}}$
instrument offset remains.
