# [edit_file] /Users/jakobfaber/Developer/repos/github.com/jakobtfaber/Faber2026/sections/toa.tex
--- /Users/jakobfaber/Developer/repos/github.com/jakobtfaber/Faber2026/sections/toa.tex
+++ /Users/jakobfaber/Developer/repos/github.com/jakobtfaber/Faber2026/sections/toa.tex
-Peak-sample TOAs on asymmetric (scattered) profiles are
-morphology-dependent; this bias is common-mode only in part, which is one
-reason the residual is a supporting diagnostic and the per-burst timing
-budget is dominated by the pulse width for broad bursts.
-
-\subsubsection{Chance-coincidence probability}
-\label{sec:toa-pcc}
+Peak-sample TOAs on asymmetric (scattered) profiles are
+morphology-dependent, and---because the scattering timescale is strongly
+frequency-dependent---this bias does not cancel between the two bands. We
+therefore adopt a model-based TOA and remove the differential scattering bias
+explicitly, as described next.
+
+\subsubsection{Time-of-arrival definition: peak sample versus model \texorpdfstring{$t_0$}{t0}}
+\label{sec:toa-definition}
+
+The peak sample of a scattered profile is not the burst arrival time. The
+observed profile in each channel is the intrinsic pulse convolved with a
+pulse-broadening function (PBF) of scattering timescale $\tau(\nu)$, and the
+convolution displaces the profile peak \emph{later} than the intrinsic
+arrival by an amount that grows monotonically with $\tau$. Because
+$\tau\propto\nu^{-\alpha}$ with $\alpha\simeq4$, CHIME (band centre
+$\sim0.6$\,GHz) is broadened far more than DSA-110 ($\sim1.4$\,GHz), so the two
+peak-sample TOAs are displaced by different amounts. Referring both bands to
+400\,MHz through Eq.~\eqref{eq:dmdelay} removes the \emph{dispersive} delay but
+leaves this \emph{scattering} displacement untouched; its inter-band difference
+propagates directly into the measured CHIME$-$DSA offset as a frequency-dependent
+systematic that the dispersive referral cannot absorb.
+
+The joint two-dimensional (time--frequency) scattering fits
+(Section~\ref{sec:jointmodel}) already provide the unbiased estimator. The
+forward model is an intrinsic Gaussian of centroid $t_0$, dispersed and then
+convolved with the PBF, so $t_0$ is the intrinsic, pre-scattering arrival time
+with the scattering tail deconvolved out. We adopt the model TOA on each band
+and quantify the correction it applies to the peak-sample TOA as the
+band-averaged peak shift of the fitted kernel,
+\begin{equation}
+\Delta_{\rm scat}(\mathrm{band}) =
+\Big\langle\, t_{\rm peak}\!\big[\,G(\sigma_\nu)\ast \mathrm{PBF}(\tau_\nu,\beta)\,\big]
+- t_0(\nu) \,\Big\rangle_\nu ,
+\label{eq:scatshift}
+\end{equation}
+where $G(\sigma_\nu)$ is the per-channel intrinsic Gaussian (width set by the
+fitted intra-channel smearing), $\mathrm{PBF}(\tau_\nu,\beta)$ is the fitted
+broadening function, and the average is weighted by the model's per-channel
+flux. The peak shift is evaluated on a dense grid and is by construction
+independent of the per-channel amplitude gains and of the residual-dispersion
+tilt across the band, so it is stable even for the multi-component bursts. Each
+band's TOA is corrected as $t_{\rm arr} = t_{\rm peak} - \Delta_{\rm scat}$
+(the peak lags the arrival, so the shift is subtracted), and the scatter-corrected
+inter-site offset is
+\begin{equation}
+\Delta t_{\rm inter\text{-}site}^{\rm model} =
+\Delta t_{\rm inter\text{-}site}^{\rm peak}
+- \big[\Delta_{\rm scat}(\mathrm{CHIME}) - \Delta_{\rm scat}(\mathrm{DSA})\big].
+\label{eq:offsetcorr}
+\end{equation}
+
+The corrections behave as the physics requires: $\Delta_{\rm scat}$ is
+non-negative, scales with $\tau$, and is always larger for CHIME than for DSA.
+Across the twelve bursts the CHIME correction ranges from $0.04$ to $3.14$\,ms
+and the DSA correction stays below $0.11$\,ms, so the differential shift
+applied to the offset is $+0.03$ to $+3.03$\,ms (median $0.20$\,ms; four bursts
+exceed $0.5$\,ms). The most heavily scattered pair, FRB~20220506D
+($\tau_{1\,\rm GHz}=0.84$\,ms), carries a $3.03$\,ms differential---its
+peak-sample offset of $+6.19$\,ms becomes $+3.16$\,ms once the scattering bias
+is removed. Because the differential is strictly positive, every offset moves
+earlier, reflecting the removal of the larger CHIME scattering delay.
+
+Adopting the model TOA also supplies a genuine localization uncertainty. The
+peak-sample budget carried only the DM-referral term
+(Eq.~\ref{eq:dmdelay} with the DM uncertainty); the joint fit instead yields a
+posterior width on $t_0$ per band, which we fold into the per-band arrival error
+in quadrature with the DM-referral term. These posterior widths are small
+($3\times10^{-4}$ to $9\times10^{-2}$\,ms), and the combined CHIME$+$DSA offset
+error remains dominated by the DSA DM-referral term ($\simeq2.42$\,ms); the
+localization contribution is significant only for the most scattered bursts
+(e.g. the CHIME arrival error of FRB~20220506D grows from $5\,\mu$s to
+$92\,\mu$s). We report the scatter-corrected offset and this folded error as
+the primary timing quantities, and retain the peak-sample offset alongside them
+for reference; the per-band corrections and both offsets are tabulated in the
+accompanying machine-readable catalog.
+
+\subsubsection{Chance-coincidence probability}
+\label{sec:toa-pcc}

import sys; sys.path.insert(0,".")
K_DM=4148.808  # MHz^2 pc^-1 cm^3 s  (F_REF=400)
f_ref=400.0; f_chime=600.39; f_dsa=1405.0
# 1) referral term per band, d(toa_400)/dDM  [ms per pc/cm3]
dch = K_DM*(1/f_ref**2 - 1/f_chime**2)*1e3
dds = K_DM*(1/f_ref**2 - 1/f_dsa**2)*1e3
print(f"d(chime_toa_400)/dDM = {dch:+.3f} ms per pc/cm3")
print(f"d(dsa_toa_400)/dDM   = {dds:+.3f} ms per pc/cm3")
# 2) SHARED-DM offset: DM enters both, so the sensitivity is the DIFFERENCE
#    (the common 1/f_ref^2 term cancels; the band-center terms do NOT)
doff_shared = K_DM*(1/f_dsa**2 - 1/f_chime**2)*1e3   # d(chime-dsa)/dDM with shared DM
print(f"\nSHARED-DM offset:  d(offset)/dDM = {doff_shared:+.3f} ms per pc/cm3")
print(f"  -> 0.1 pc/cm3 shifts the offset by {abs(doff_shared)*0.1:.3f} ms")
# 3) if CHIME used its OWN DM and DSA its own (per-telescope), a DM DIFFERENCE dDM
#    tilts only the CHIME referral -> full CHIME referral slope acts on dDM
print(f"\nPER-TELESCOPE dDM (CHIME re-referred with its own DM):")
print(f"  0.117 pc/cm3 (max band offset) -> {abs(dch)*0.117:.2f} ms CHIME shift")

def calculate_dm_timing_error(dDM, f_obs, f_ref, K_DM_value: float = K_DM):
    """Calculate timing error due to DM uncertainty."""
    time_shift = K_DM_value * dDM * (1 / f_obs.value**2 - 1 / f_ref.value**2) * u.s
    return np.abs(time_shift.to(u.ms))


def clean_and_serialize_dict(burst_dict: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:
    """Convert astropy objects in a dictionary to JSON-serializable types."""
    clean_dict: Dict[str, Any] = {}
    for key, value in burst_dict.items():
        if isinstance(value, u.Quantity):
            clean_dict[key] = value.value
        elif isinstance(value, Time):
            clean_dict[key] = value.iso
        else:
            clean_dict[key] = value
    return clean_dict


def append_to_json(new_data_dict: Dict[str, Any], filename: str) -> None:
    """Append a dictionary to a JSON file, creating the file if needed."""
    clean_new_data = clean_and_serialize_dict(new_data_dict)
    if os.path.exists(filename) and os.path.getsize(filename) > 0:
        try:
            with open(filename, "r") as f:
                data_list = json.load(f)

stdout:
=== full band_result + peak_shift ===
def peak_shift(sig, tau, beta):
    """Peak position of G(0,sig) (x) PBF(tau,beta) on a dense grid. Array-shaped calls."""
    sig=float(max(sig,1e-6)); tau=float(max(tau,1e-6))
    span=max(20*tau, 12*sig, 2.0); dt=min(sig,tau)/50.0; dt=max(dt, span/400000)
    tg=np.arange(-span, span, dt)
    mu=np.array([[0.0]]); S=np.array([[sig]]); T=np.array([[tau]])
    bpbf=float(np.clip(beta, BETA_THIN_SCREEN_MIN, BETA_THIN_SCREEN_MAX))
    if bpbf >= BETA_THIN_SCREEN_MAX - BETA_EXP_EPS:
        y=_agec(tg, mu, S, T)[0]
    else:
        y=_gpc(tg, mu, S, T, bpbf)[0]
    i=int(np.argmax(y))
    if 0<i<len(y)-1:
        d=y[i-1]-2*y[i]+y[i+1]; off=0.5*(y[i-1]-y[i+1])/d if d!=0 else 0.0
    else: off=0.0
    return float(tg[i]+off*dt)

def band_result(b, band):
    fit,npz,vb=R2.sources(b); d=json.load(open(fit)); p={k:v["median"] for k,v in d["percentiles"].items()}
    perr={k:(abs(v.get("err_minus",0.))+abs(v.get("err_plus",0.)))/2 for k,v in d["percentiles"].items()}
    tel="chime" if band=="C" else "dsa"
    z=np.load(npz); t=np.asarray(z[f"time{band}"],float); freq=np.asarray(z[f"freq{band}"],float)
    npzm=np.asarray(z[f"model{band}"],float); data=np.asarray(z[f"data{band}"],float)
    noise=z[f"noise{band}"]; dt=float(np.diff(t)[0])
    tau=float(z["tau_1ghz"]); beta=float(z["beta"])
    m=FRBModel(t,freq,data=npzm,dm_init=R2._cfg_dm_init(b,tel),df_MHz=R2.NATIVE_DF[tel],beta=beta,noise_std=noise)
    pscat,_,_,n=R2._params(d,p,band,freq,tau_override=tau,beta_override=beta)
    # collapsed peak (authoritative model, fall back to data if flat)
    prof_m=np.nansum(npzm,0); use_model=(prof_m.max()-prof_m.min())/(abs(prof_m.max())+1e-30)>=0.05
    src=npzm if use_model else data; prof=np.nansum(src,0)
    P_scat=t[0]+R2.parpeak(prof,int(np.argmax(prof)))*dt
    data_peak=float(t[int(np.argmax(np.nansum(data,0)))])
    # dominant component: t0 nearest the collapsed peak
    t0s={i:pscat[i-1].t0 for i in range(1,n+1)}
    dom=min(t0s,key=lambda i:abs(t0s[i]-P_scat)); par=pscat[dom-1]
    alpha=par.alpha
    tau_f=np.clip(tau*(freq/1.0)**(-alpha),1e-6,None)
    sig_f=np.atleast_1d(m._smearing_sigma(m.dm_init+par.delta_dm, par.zeta)).astype(float)
    if sig_f.size==1: sig_f=np.full(len(freq),float(sig_f))
    sig_f=np.clip(sig_f,1e-6,None)
    chflux=np.clip(np.nansum(src,1),0,None); ok=chflux>0
    ps=np.array([peak_shift(sig_f[fi],tau_f[fi],beta) for fi in range(len(freq))])
    Delta_scat=float(np.sum(chflux[ok]*ps[ok])/np.sum(chflux[ok]))
    # per-channel tau/sig band-center summaries
    tau_cen=float(np.sum(chflux[ok]*tau_f[ok])/np.sum(chflux[ok]))
    # localization error: dominant-component t0 posterior half-width
    if d.get("shared_zeta") or f"t0_{band}" in p:
        loc=perr.get(f"t0_{band}",0.0)
    else:
        loc=perr.get(f"t0_{band}{dom}",0.0)
    intrinsic_toa=P_scat-Delta_scat
    return dict(burst=b,band=tel,verdict_burst=vb,n_comp=n,dominant_comp=dom,
                tau_1ghz=tau,beta=beta,alpha=float(alpha),tau_center_ms=tau_cen,
                collapsed_peak_ms=P_scat,data_peak_ms=data_peak,
                scatter_corr_ms=Delta_scat,intrinsic_toa_ms=intrinsic_toa,
                loc_err_ms=float(loc),used_model=bool(use_model),dt_ms=dt,
                final=R2.VMAP[vb]["final"])

if __name__=="__main__":
    import sys as s; print(band_result(s.argv[1],s.argv[2]))

import sys; sys.path.insert(0,".")
import json, numpy as np
import astropy.units as u
from astropy.coordinates import SkyCoord, EarthLocation
from astropy.time import Time
import astropy.constants as const
from crossmatching.toa_crossmatch import DRAO_LOCATION, OVRO_LOCATION, compute_geometric_delay, crossmatch_input_from_dict

fix=json.load(open("crossmatching/notebook_reproduction_fixture.json"))
# baseline light-crossing
p1=DRAO_LOCATION.get_gcrs(Time(60000,format="mjd")).cartesian.xyz
p2=OVRO_LOCATION.get_gcrs(Time(60000,format="mjd")).cartesian.xyz
base=np.linalg.norm((p2-p1).to_value(u.m)); print("baseline |p2-p1| = %.1f km, max |tau_geo|=%.3f ms"%(base/1e3, base/const.c.value*1e3))

print("\nPer-burst dtau_geo/dtheta (finite diff, perturb source position by 1 arcsec):")
print(f"{'burst':11s}{'coeff_us/arcsec':>16s}{'term@1arcsec_us':>16s}{'term@1arcmin_us':>16s}")
arc=(1*u.arcsec).to_value(u.rad)
for row in fix["bursts"]:
    inp=crossmatch_input_from_dict(row)
    src=SkyCoord(row["source_coord"], unit=(u.hourangle,u.deg), frame="icrs")
    t=inp.chime.toa_time_400
    g0=compute_geometric_delay(t,src,DRAO_LOCATION,OVRO_LOCATION).to_value(u.ms)
    # perturb in RA and Dec by 1 arcsec, take max sensitivity
    dmax=0.0
    for dra,ddec in [(1/np.cos(src.dec.rad),0),(0,1)]:
        s2=SkyCoord(src.ra+dra*u.arcsec, src.dec+ddec*u.arcsec, frame="icrs")
        g1=compute_geometric_delay(t,s2,DRAO_LOCATION,OVRO_LOCATION).to_value(u.ms)
        dmax=max(dmax,abs(g1-g0))
    print(f"{row['name']:11s}{dmax*1e3:16.4f}{dmax*1e3:16.4f}{dmax*60*1e3:16.3f}")

import sys; sys.path.insert(0,"/Users/jakobfaber/handoffs/toa_model"); sys.path.insert(0,".")
sys.path.insert(0,"scattering")
import json, numpy as np, gc
import toa_recon2 as R2
from scat_analysis.burstfit import (FRBModel, BETA_THIN_SCREEN_MIN, BETA_THIN_SCREEN_MAX, BETA_EXP_EPS,
                                     analytic_gaussian_exp_convolution as _agec,
                                     gaussian_powerlaw_convolution as _gpc)

def peak_shift(sig,tau,beta):
    sig=float(max(sig,1e-6)); tau=float(max(tau,1e-6))
    span=max(20*tau,12*sig,2.0); dt=min(sig,tau)/50.0; dt=max(dt,span/400000)
    tg=np.arange(-span,span,dt); mu=np.array([[0.0]]); S=np.array([[sig]]); T=np.array([[tau]])
    bpbf=float(np.clip(beta,BETA_THIN_SCREEN_MIN,BETA_THIN_SCREEN_MAX))
    y=(_agec(tg,mu,S,T)[0] if bpbf>=BETA_THIN_SCREEN_MAX-BETA_EXP_EPS else _gpc(tg,mu,S,T,bpbf)[0])
    i=int(np.argmax(y))
    if 0<i<len(y)-1:
        d=y[i-1]-2*y[i]+y[i+1]; off=0.5*(y[i-1]-y[i+1])/d if d!=0 else 0.0
    else: off=0.0
    return float(tg[i]+off*dt)

def band_ext(b,band):
    fit,npz,vb=R2.sources(b); d=json.load(open(fit))
    p={k:v["median"] for k,v in d["percentiles"].items()}
    perr={k:(abs(v.get("err_minus",0.))+abs(v.get("err_plus",0.)))/2 for k,v in d["percentiles"].items()}
    tel="chime" if band=="C" else "dsa"
    z=np.load(npz); t=np.asarray(z[f"time{band}"],float); freq=np.asarray(z[f"freq{band}"],float)
    npzm=np.asarray(z[f"model{band}"],float); data=np.asarray(z[f"data{band}"],float)
    noise=z[f"noise{band}"]; tau=float(z["tau_1ghz"]); beta=float(z["beta"])
    m=FRBModel(t,freq,data=npzm,dm_init=R2._cfg_dm_init(b,tel),df_MHz=R2.NATIVE_DF[tel],beta=beta,noise_std=noise)
    pscat,_,_,n=R2._params(d,p,band,freq,tau_override=tau,beta_override=beta)
    prof_m=np.nansum(npzm,0); use_model=(prof_m.max()-prof_m.min())/(abs(prof_m.max())+1e-30)>=0.05
    src=npzm if use_model else data; prof=np.nansum(src,0); dt=float(np.diff(t)[0])
    P_scat=t[0]+R2.parpeak(prof,int(np.argmax(prof)))*dt
    t0s={i:pscat[i-1].t0 for i in range(1,n+1)}
    dom=min(t0s,key=lambda i:abs(t0s[i]-P_scat)); par=pscat[dom-1]; alpha=par.alpha
    chflux=np.clip(np.nansum(src,1),0,None); ok=chflux>0; w=chflux[ok]/np.sum(chflux[ok])
    def dscat(tau_,beta_):
        tau_f=np.clip(tau_*(freq/1.0)**(-alpha),1e-6,None)
        sig_f=np.atleast_1d(m._smearing_sigma(m.dm_init+par.delta_dm,par.zeta)).astype(float)
        if sig_f.size==1: sig_f=np.full(len(freq),float(sig_f))
        sig_f=np.clip(sig_f,1e-6,None)
        ps=np.array([peak_shift(sig_f[fi],tau_f[fi],beta_) for fi in range(len(freq))])
        return float(np.sum(w*ps[ok]))
    D0=dscat(tau,beta)
    # (3) sigma on Delta_scat from tau + beta posteriors (fractional tau uncert from JSON applied to NPZ tau)
    tau_med=p.get("tau_1ghz",tau); sig_tau_frac=perr.get("tau_1ghz",0.0)/max(tau_med,1e-9)
    sig_tau=sig_tau_frac*tau
    dD_dtau=(dscat(tau*1.01,beta)-dscat(tau*0.99,beta))/(0.02*tau)
    term_tau=abs(dD_dtau)*sig_tau
    # beta: exp-PBF (beta>=3.98) is pinned -> ~0; else use JSON sigma_beta or convert sigma_alpha
    if beta < BETA_THIN_SCREEN_MAX-BETA_EXP_EPS:
        if "beta" in perr: sig_beta=perr["beta"]
        elif "alpha" in perr:  # alpha=2b/(b-2) -> dalpha/db=-4/(b-2)^2 -> sig_beta=sig_alpha*(b-2)^2/4
            sig_beta=perr["alpha"]*(beta-2)**2/4.0
        else: sig_beta=0.0
        db=max(0.01,0.5*sig_beta)
        dD_db=(dscat(tau,min(beta+db,BETA_THIN_SCREEN_MAX-BETA_EXP_EPS-1e-3))-dscat(tau,beta-db))/(2*db) if sig_beta>0 else 0.0
        term_beta=abs(dD_db)*sig_beta
    else:
        sig_beta=0.0; term_beta=0.0
    sig_Dscat=float(np.hypot(term_tau,term_beta))
    # (7) multi-component ambiguity: flux-weighted std of component t0s
    if n>1:
        # weight each component by its integrated model flux (use t0-proximity gain proxy: equal weight fallback)
        t0v=np.array([pscat[i].t0 for i in range(n)])
        comp_ambig=float(np.std(t0v))  # spread of candidate arrival definitions
    else:
        comp_ambig=0.0
    z.close(); del z,npzm,data,src,prof_m; gc.collect()
    return dict(burst=b,band=tel,n=n,dom=dom,tau=tau,beta=beta,
                sig_tau=sig_tau,term_tau=term_tau,sig_beta=sig_beta,term_beta=term_beta,
                sig_Dscat=sig_Dscat,comp_ambig=comp_ambig,D0=D0)

BURSTS=["casey","chromatica","freya","hamilton","isha","johndoeII","mahi","oran","phineas","whitney","wilhelm","zach"]
out={}
print(f"{'burst':11s}{'bnd':4s}{'n':>2s}{'Dscat':>8s}{'sTau':>8s}{'sBeta':>8s}{'sDscat':>8s}{'compAmb':>9s}")
for b in BURSTS:
    out[b]={}
    for band in ["C","D"]:
        try:
            r=band_ext(b,band); out[b][band]=r
            print(f"{b:11s}{r['band']:4s}{r['n']:2d}{r['D0']:8.4f}{r['term_tau']:8.4f}{r['term_beta']:8.4f}{r['sig_Dscat']:8.4f}{r['comp_ambig']:9.4f}")
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"{b:11s}{band:4s} ERR {type(e).__name__}: {str(e)[:60]}")
json.dump(out, open("/Users/jakobfaber/handoffs/toa_model/error_terms_extra.json","w"), indent=2, default=float)
print("\nwrote error_terms_extra.json")
PYEOF

import sys; sys.path.insert(0,"/Users/jakobfaber/handoffs/toa_model"); sys.path.insert(0,".")
sys.path.insert(0,"scattering")
import json, numpy as np, gc
import toa_recon2 as R2
from scat_analysis.burstfit import FRBModel

def comp_ambiguity(b,band):
    """Flux-weighted spread of candidate component-t0 arrivals, weighted by the
    observed collapsed-profile amplitude at each component's location. Dominant
    single component -> ~0; genuinely comparable separated components -> their
    flux-weighted separation."""
    fit,npz,vb=R2.sources(b); d=json.load(open(fit))
    p={k:v["median"] for k,v in d["percentiles"].items()}
    z=np.load(npz); t=np.asarray(z[f"time{band}"],float); freq=np.asarray(z[f"freq{band}"],float)
    npzm=np.asarray(z[f"model{band}"],float); data=np.asarray(z[f"data{band}"],float)
    noise=z[f"noise{band}"]; tau=float(z["tau_1ghz"]); beta=float(z["beta"])
    tel="chime" if band=="C" else "dsa"
    m=FRBModel(t,freq,data=npzm,dm_init=R2._cfg_dm_init(b,tel),df_MHz=R2.NATIVE_DF[tel],beta=beta,noise_std=noise)
    pscat,_,_,n=R2._params(d,p,band,freq,tau_override=tau,beta_override=beta)
    prof_m=np.nansum(npzm,0); use_model=(prof_m.max()-prof_m.min())/(abs(prof_m.max())+1e-30)>=0.05
    prof=np.nansum(npzm if use_model else data,0); prof=np.clip(prof,0,None)
    t0v=np.array([pscat[i].t0 for i in range(n)])
    # weight = observed flux at each component's t0 (nearest sample)
    idx=np.clip(np.searchsorted(t,t0v),0,len(t)-1)
    w=prof[idx]; 
    if w.sum()<=0 or n==1:
        amb=0.0
    else:
        w=w/w.sum(); mean=np.sum(w*t0v); amb=float(np.sqrt(np.sum(w*(t0v-mean)**2)))
    z.close(); del z,npzm,data,prof_m,prof; gc.collect()
    return n, float(amb), t0v.tolist(), (w.tolist() if n>1 and 'w' in dir() else [])

BURSTS=["casey","chromatica","freya","hamilton","isha","johndoeII","mahi","oran","phineas","whitney","wilhelm","zach"]
extra=json.load(open("/Users/jakobfaber/handoffs/toa_model/error_terms_extra.json"))
print(f"{'burst':11s}{'bnd':4s}{'n':>2s}{'old_std':>9s}{'flux_wtd':>9s}")
for b in BURSTS:
    for band in ["C","D"]:
        n,amb,t0v,w=comp_ambiguity(b,band)
        old=extra[b][band]["comp_ambig"]
        extra[b][band]["comp_ambig"]=amb  # overwrite with flux-weighted
        tel="chime" if band=="C" else "dsa"
        if n>1: print(f"{b:11s}{tel:4s}{n:2d}{old:9.3f}{amb:9.4f}")
json.dump(extra, open("/Users/jakobfaber/handoffs/toa_model/error_terms_extra.json","w"), indent=2, default=float)
print("\nupdated comp_ambig -> flux-weighted in error_terms_extra.json")

import sys; sys.path.insert(0,".")
import json, numpy as np, pandas as pd
import astropy.units as u
from astropy.coordinates import SkyCoord
from crossmatching.toa_crossmatch import DRAO_LOCATION, OVRO_LOCATION, compute_geometric_delay, crossmatch_input_from_dict

fix=json.load(open("crossmatching/notebook_reproduction_fixture.json"))
extra=json.load(open("/Users/jakobfaber/handoffs/toa_model/error_terms_extra.json"))
tbl=pd.read_csv("/Users/jakobfaber/handoffs/toa_model/toa_scatter_correction_table.csv").set_index("burst")

NOMINAL_LOC_ARCSEC=3.0  # DSA-110 localization scale used for the geometric term
rows=[]
for row in fix["bursts"]:
    b=row["name"]; inp=crossmatch_input_from_dict(row)
    src=SkyCoord(row["source_coord"], unit=(u.hourangle,u.deg), frame="icrs")
    t=inp.chime.toa_time_400
    g0=compute_geometric_delay(t,src,DRAO_LOCATION,OVRO_LOCATION).to_value(u.ms)
    dmax=0.0
    for dra,ddec in [(1/np.cos(src.dec.rad),0),(0,1)]:
        s2=SkyCoord(src.ra+dra*u.arcsec, src.dec+ddec*u.arcsec, frame="icrs")
        g1=compute_geometric_delay(t,s2,DRAO_LOCATION,OVRO_LOCATION).to_value(u.ms)
        dmax=max(dmax,abs(g1-g0))
    geo_pos_err=dmax*NOMINAL_LOC_ARCSEC  # ms
    eC=extra[b]["C"]; eD=extra[b]["D"]
    rows.append(dict(burst=b,
        sig_dscat_C_ms=eC["sig_Dscat"], sig_dscat_D_ms=eD["sig_Dscat"],
        comp_ambig_C_ms=eC["comp_ambig"], comp_ambig_D_ms=eD["comp_ambig"],
        geo_pos_err_ms=geo_pos_err))
ext=pd.DataFrame(rows).set_index("burst")
T=tbl.join(ext)

# build full per-band errors folding (3)+(7) into the existing model errors (which already have dm_referral + t0_loc)
T["error_chime_full_ms"]=np.sqrt(T["error_chime_model_ms"]**2 + T["sig_dscat_C_ms"]**2 + T["comp_ambig_C_ms"]**2)
T["error_dsa_full_ms"]  =np.sqrt(T["error_dsa_model_ms"]**2  + T["sig_dscat_D_ms"]**2 + T["comp_ambig_D_ms"]**2)
# combined offset error also folds the inter-site geometric-position term (9)
T["combined_err_full_ms"]=np.sqrt(T["error_chime_full_ms"]**2 + T["error_dsa_full_ms"]**2 + T["geo_pos_err_ms"]**2)

cols=["error_chime_model_ms","sig_dscat_C_ms","comp_ambig_C_ms","error_chime_full_ms",
      "error_dsa_model_ms","comp_ambig_D_ms","error_dsa_full_ms","geo_pos_err_ms",
      "combined_err_model_ms","combined_err_full_ms"]
pd.set_option("display.width",200,"display.max_columns",30)
print(T[cols].round(4).to_string())
T.reset_index().to_csv("/Users/jakobfaber/handoffs/toa_model/toa_scatter_correction_table.csv",index=False)
print("\ncols now:", T.reset_index().shape[1])
print("geo_pos_err range (µs):", round(T.geo_pos_err_ms.min()*1e3,3),"to",round(T.geo_pos_err_ms.max()*1e3,3))
PYEOF

--- /Users/jakobfaber/Developer/repos/github.com/jakobtfaber/dsa110-FLITS/tests/test_crossmatching_notebook_reproduction.py
+++ /Users/jakobfaber/Developer/repos/github.com/jakobtfaber/dsa110-FLITS/tests/test_crossmatching_notebook_reproduction.py
-        # Folded error budget: combined = hypot(chime_model, dsa_model).
-        assert result["combined_error_ms"] == pytest.approx(
-            (result["error_chime_ms"] ** 2 + result["error_dsa_ms"] ** 2) ** 0.5,
-            abs=1e-9,
-        )
-        # Scattering peak-shift is non-negative and CHIME >= DSA.
-        assert result["scatter_corr_chime_ms"] >= 0.0
-        assert result["scatter_corr_dsa_ms"] >= 0.0
-        assert result["differential_scatter_shift_ms"] >= -1e-9
+        # Folded error budget: combined = hypot(chime_model, dsa_model).
+        assert result["combined_error_ms"] == pytest.approx(
+            (result["error_chime_ms"] ** 2 + result["error_dsa_ms"] ** 2) ** 0.5,
+            abs=1e-9,
+        )
+        # Scattering peak-shift is non-negative and CHIME >= DSA.
+        assert result["scatter_corr_chime_ms"] >= 0.0
+        assert result["scatter_corr_dsa_ms"] >= 0.0
+        assert result["differential_scatter_shift_ms"] >= -1e-9
+
+        # Extended budget: full per-band error = quadrature of the model error,
+        # the scatter-correction uncertainty, and the multi-component ambiguity.
+        assert result["error_chime_full_ms"] == pytest.approx(
+            (
+                result["error_chime_ms"] ** 2
+                + result["scatter_corr_unc_chime_ms"] ** 2
+                + result["comp_ambig_chime_ms"] ** 2
+            )
+            ** 0.5,
+            abs=1e-9,
+        )
+        assert result["error_dsa_full_ms"] == pytest.approx(
+            (
+                result["error_dsa_ms"] ** 2
+                + result["scatter_corr_unc_dsa_ms"] ** 2
+                + result["comp_ambig_dsa_ms"] ** 2
+            )
+            ** 0.5,
+            abs=1e-9,
+        )
+        # Combined-full folds both full per-band errors and the inter-site
+        # geometric-position term.
+        assert result["combined_error_full_ms"] == pytest.approx(
+            (
+                result["error_chime_full_ms"] ** 2
+                + result["error_dsa_full_ms"] ** 2
+                + result["geo_pos_err_ms"] ** 2
+            )
+            ** 0.5,
+            abs=1e-9,
+        )
+        # Extended terms are non-negative and never shrink the budget.
+        for key in (
+            "scatter_corr_unc_chime_ms",
+            "scatter_corr_unc_dsa_ms",
+            "comp_ambig_chime_ms",
+            "comp_ambig_dsa_ms",
+            "geo_pos_err_ms",
+        ):
+            assert result[key] >= 0.0
+        assert result["error_chime_full_ms"] >= result["error_chime_ms"] - 1e-9
+        assert result["error_dsa_full_ms"] >= result["error_dsa_ms"] - 1e-9
+        assert result["combined_error_full_ms"] >= result["combined_error_ms"] - 1e-9

# [edit_file] /Users/jakobfaber/Developer/repos/github.com/jakobtfaber/Faber2026/sections/toa.tex
--- /Users/jakobfaber/Developer/repos/github.com/jakobtfaber/Faber2026/sections/toa.tex
+++ /Users/jakobfaber/Developer/repos/github.com/jakobtfaber/Faber2026/sections/toa.tex
-the primary timing quantities, and retain the peak-sample offset alongside them
-for reference; the per-band corrections and both offsets are tabulated in the
-accompanying machine-readable catalog.
-
-\subsubsection{Chance-coincidence probability}
+the primary timing quantities, and retain the peak-sample offset alongside them
+for reference; the per-band corrections and both offsets are tabulated in the
+accompanying machine-readable catalog.
+
+Beyond the DM-referral and $t_0$-localization terms, three further
+contributions enter the per-burst arrival-time budget, and we account for each
+explicitly. First, the scattering correction $\Delta_{\rm scat}$ itself carries
+an uncertainty propagated from the $\tau$ and $\beta$ posteriors of the joint
+fit; this term is largest for the most heavily scattered sightline
+(FRB~20220506D, $0.10$\,ms) and negligible ($\lesssim0.01$\,ms) elsewhere.
+Second, for the multi-component bursts the arrival time depends on which
+component is taken to define it; we quantify this ambiguity as the flux-weighted
+spread of the fitted component centroids, weighting each candidate by the
+observed profile amplitude at its location so that a decisively dominant
+component contributes negligibly and only genuinely comparable, separated
+components inflate the budget. This term is zero for the six single-component
+bursts and dominates the budget where components overlap in flux
+(FRB~20220307B $2.99$\,ms, FRB~20221029A $1.90$\,ms). Third, the inter-site
+geometric delay depends on the source position through the projection onto the
+$1345$\,km DRAO--OVRO baseline; at the arcsecond localization of DSA-110 this
+term is only $\sim0.06\,\mu$s and is included for completeness rather than
+significance. Folding the scatter-correction and component-ambiguity terms into
+the per-band errors, and the geometric-position term into the combined offset
+error, leaves the six clean single-component bursts unchanged at the
+DM-dominated $\simeq2.42$\,ms but raises the combined offset error for the
+multi-component bursts by factors of $1.15$--$1.60$ (e.g. FRB~20220307B to
+$3.88$\,ms, FRB~20221029A to $3.08$\,ms). The multi-component ambiguity---not
+scattering or dispersion---is therefore the dominant systematic on the timing
+consistency of the blended bursts, and the association significance
+(Section~\ref{sec:toa-pcc}) uses the full folded error as its coincidence
+tolerance.
+
+\subsubsection{Chance-coincidence probability}

def chime_toa_shift_ms(
    dsa: BandSpectrum, chime: BandSpectrum, measured_offset_ms: float
) -> float:
    """Shift CHIME cube times so its peak is ``measured_offset_ms`` after DSA peak."""
    return band_peak_time(dsa) + measured_offset_ms - band_peak_time(chime)

def toa_offset_ms(burst: str, toa_json: Path | None = None) -> float | None:
    """Offset used to align the two bands' *observed* cube peaks for display.

    This is the peak-based CHIME-DSA offset, not the scatter-corrected model
    offset: the codetection gallery overlays the observed (scattered) profiles,
    so their peaks must be aligned. Since the model switch, the golden JSON's
    ``measured_offset_ms`` is the intrinsic (de-scattered) offset and the peak
    offset lives in ``peak_measured_offset_ms``. Prefer the peak key; fall back
    to ``measured_offset_ms`` for older JSONs that predate the model extension.
    """
    fp = toa_json or _TOA_JSON
    if not fp.exists():
        return None
    rows = json.loads(fp.read_text())
    row = rows.get(burst) or rows.get(burst.lower())
    if not row:
        return None
    offset = row.get("peak_measured_offset_ms", row.get("measured_offset_ms"))
    return float(offset) if offset is not None else None

=== fit_toa_shift_ms + how the shift is chosen (lines 112-190) ===
def _register_fit_grid_ms(
    fit_t: np.ndarray, fit_prof: np.ndarray, native_prof: np.ndarray, dt_native_ms: float
) -> float:
    """Native-frame time (ms) of the fit grid's first sample.

    The fit-delivery grid is a crop+downsample of the same archival stream, so
    cross-correlating its band profile against the native archival profile
    (downsampled to the fit dt) registers the two frames exactly, using the
    whole profile rather than a single noisy peak sample.
    """
    dt_fit = float(np.median(np.diff(fit_t)))
    r = int(round(dt_fit / dt_native_ms))
    if r < 1 or abs(dt_fit - r * dt_native_ms) > 0.02 * dt_fit:
        raise ValueError(f"fit dt {dt_fit} is not a multiple of native dt {dt_native_ms}")
    arch = _standardize(_block_mean_1d(native_prof, r))
    fit = _standardize(fit_prof)
    if fit.size > arch.size:
        raise ValueError("fit grid longer than archival window")
    lag = int(np.argmax(np.correlate(arch, fit, mode="valid")))
    return lag * r * dt_native_ms


def fit_toa_shift_ms(
    row: dict, *, root: Path, data_root: Path, target_dm: float
) -> dict[str, float]:
    """Per-band shift (band label -> ms) moving the display anchor from the
    data profile peak to the fitted arrival time from the accepted joint fit:
    the t0 (referenced to the top of each band) of the model component nearest
    the model profile peak, i.e. the dominant component.

    Empty for rows without an accepted joint fit (peak anchor kept).
    """
    npz_rel = row.get("npz")
    if not npz_rel:
        return {}
    npz_path = root / npz_rel
    from plot_codetection_triptych import dominant_t0_ms, fit_json_path

    percentiles = json.loads(fit_json_path(npz_path).read_text())["percentiles"]
    z = np.load(npz_path, allow_pickle=True)

    from plot_codetection_gallery import BANDS, FILE_NICK, discover_products, load_band

    file_nick = FILE_NICK.get(row["nick"], row["nick"])
    products = discover_products(data_root, file_nick)

    shifts: dict[str, float] = {}
    for band_key, tel, label in (("C", "chime", "CHIME/FRB"), ("D", "dsa", "DSA-110")):
        fit_t = np.asarray(z[f"time{band_key}"], float)
        with warnings.catch_warnings():
            warnings.simplefilter("ignore", category=RuntimeWarning)
            fit_prof = np.nansum(np.asarray(z[f"data{band_key}"], float), axis=0)
            model_prof = np.nansum(np.asarray(z[f"model{band_key}"], float), axis=0)
        t0 = dominant_t0_ms(percentiles, band_key, fit_t, model_prof)

        # Register against the original archival stream because the fit NPZ
        # was made at the product's filename-stem DM. Compute the display
        # anchor separately after applying the same adopted-DM correction as
        # bands_archival; re-dedispersion changes the band-summed peak.
        f_factor, t_factor_disp = DISPLAY_FACTORS[tel]
        dt_native = BANDS[tel]["dt_ms"]
        band_native = dict(BANDS[tel], f_factor=f_factor, t_factor=1)
        _, native_prof = load_band(products[tel].path, band_native)
        band_disp = dict(BANDS[tel], f_factor=f_factor, t_factor=t_factor_disp)
        residual_dm = float(target_dm - products[tel].dm)
        _, disp_prof = load_band(
            products[tel].path,
            band_disp,
            telescope=tel,
            residual_dm=residual_dm,
        )
        pk_native_ms = int(np.nanargmax(disp_prof)) * t_factor_disp * dt_native

        start_native_ms = _register_fit_grid_ms(fit_t, fit_prof, native_prof, dt_native)
        toa_display_ms = start_native_ms + (t0 - float(fit_t[0])) - pk_native_ms
        shifts[label] = -toa_display_ms
    return shifts

=== render_grid + main: how is the shift anchor selected? (lines 344-460) ===
def render_grid(
    rows: list[dict],
    *,
    root: Path,
    data_root: Path,
    out: Path,
    dpi: int,
    dm_catalog: Path = DM_CATALOG_DEFAULT,
) -> None:
    adopted_dms = load_adopted_dms(dm_catalog)
    roster = {row["nick"].lower() for row in rows}
    if roster != set(adopted_dms):
        raise ValueError("manifest and adopted-DM catalog rosters differ")
    _apply_style()
    plt.rcParams.update(
        {
            "font.size": 7,
            "axes.labelsize": 7,
            "xtick.labelsize": 6,
            "ytick.labelsize": 6,
            "axes.linewidth": 0.6,
            "xtick.direction": "in",
            "ytick.direction": "in",
        }
    )
    # At \textwidth the typeset height plus the combined adopted-DM/fitted-TOA
    # caption must fit on one AASTeX float page.
    fig = plt.figure(figsize=(7.3, 7.62))
    outer = fig.add_gridspec(
        4, 3, hspace=0.2, wspace=0.14, left=0.065, right=0.995, top=0.978, bottom=0.042
    )
    for index, row in enumerate(rows):
        bands = load_row_bands(
            row,
            root=root,
            data_root=data_root,
            target_dm=adopted_dms[row["nick"].lower()],
        )
        fmap = _gap_display_map(bands)
        cell = outer[index // 3, index % 3].subgridspec(
            2, 2, width_ratios=[3.4, 1.0], height_ratios=[1.0, 4.1],
            wspace=0.07, hspace=0.09,
        )
        ax_prof = fig.add_subplot(cell[0, 0])
        ax_wf = fig.add_subplot(cell[1, 0])
        ax_sp = fig.add_subplot(cell[1, 1], sharey=ax_wf)

        draw_joint_waterfall(ax_wf, bands, title="", fmap=fmap)
        ax_prof.set_title(row["tns"], fontsize=7, pad=2)
        draw_profile_strip(ax_prof, bands, ax_wf.get_xlim())
        draw_spectrum_marginal(ax_sp, bands, _band_gap_mhz(bands), fmap=fmap)

        yticks = [500, 700, 1400]
        ax_wf.set_yticks([float(fmap(v)) for v in yticks])
        ax_wf.set_yticklabels([str(v) for v in yticks])
        if index % 3 == 0:
            ax_wf.set_ylabel("Frequency (MHz)", fontsize=7)
        else:
            ax_wf.tick_params(labelleft=False)
        if index // 3 == 3:
            ax_wf.set_xlabel("Time (ms)", fontsize=7)
        if index == 0:
            ax_prof.legend(
                fontsize=5, frameon=False, loc="upper right", handlelength=1.0,
                borderaxespad=0.1, labelspacing=0.2,
            )
    out.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    fig.savefig(out.with_suffix(".png"), dpi=dpi)
    fig.savefig(out.with_suffix(".pdf"), dpi=dpi)
    fig.savefig(out.with_suffix(".svg"), dpi=dpi)
    plt.close(fig)


def main() -> int:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=__doc__)
    parser.add_argument("--manifest", type=Path, default=MANIFEST_DEFAULT)
    parser.add_argument("--data-root", type=Path, default=DATA_ROOT_DEFAULT)
    parser.add_argument("--out", type=Path, default=OUT_DEFAULT)
    parser.add_argument("--dm-catalog", type=Path, default=DM_CATALOG_DEFAULT)
    parser.add_argument("--dpi", type=int, default=600)
    args = parser.parse_args()
    rows = load_manifest(args.manifest)
    render_grid(
        rows,
        root=ROOT,
        data_root=args.data_root,
        out=args.out,
        dpi=args.dpi,
        dm_catalog=args.dm_catalog,
    )
    print(f"wrote {args.out.with_suffix('.pdf')}")
    return 0


if __name__ == "__main__":
    raise SystemExit(main())

def bands_archival(
    data_root: Path,
    nick: str,
    factors: dict[str, tuple[int, int]] | None = None,
    pad_scale: float = 1.0,
    pad_cap_ms: float | None = None,
    target_dm: float | None = None,
    extra_shift_ms: dict[str, float] | None = None,
) -> list[BandSpectrum]:
    """Archival `_cntr_bpc.npy` products as BandSpectrum pairs (no model).

    `factors` overrides the gallery display resolution per telescope as
    (f_factor, t_factor) block-averaging factors of the native grid.
    `pad_scale` scales and `pad_cap_ms` bounds the CHIME-width display
    padding around the on-pulse union.
    When ``target_dm`` is supplied, each native waterfall is shifted from its
    filename-stem DM to that common value before any display averaging.
    `extra_shift_ms` (band label -> ms) is applied after the TOA alignment
    and before windowing, e.g. to move the per-band anchor from the data
    profile peak to a fitted arrival time.
    """
    file_nick = FILE_NICK.get(nick, nick)
    products = discover_products(data_root, file_nick)
    out: list[BandSpectrum] = []
    for tel in ("chime", "dsa"):
        band = dict(BANDS[tel])
        if factors and tel in factors:
            band["f_factor"], band["t_factor"] = factors[tel]
        residual_dm = 0.0 if target_dm is None else float(target_dm - products[tel].dm)
        ds, profile = load_band(
            products[tel].path,
            band,
            telescope=tel,
            residual_dm=residual_dm,
        )
        n_f, n_t = ds.shape
        dt = band["dt_ms"] * band["t_factor"]
        pk = int(np.nanargmax(profile))
        t = (np.arange(n_t) - pk) * dt
        f_mhz = np.linspace(band["f_lo"], band["f_hi"], n_f) * 1e3
        label = "CHIME/FRB" if tel == "chime" else "DSA-110"
        out.append(
            BandSpectrum(
                freq_mhz=f_mhz,
                time_ms=t,
                data=ds,
                model=np.zeros_like(ds),
                sigma=_sigma_from_offpulse(ds),
                label=label,
                channel_valid=_channel_valid(ds),
            )
        )
    fine = _align_toa(out, nick, target_dm=target_dm)
    if _toa_offset(nick, target_dm=target_dm) is None:
        chime, dsa = fine
        fine = [_shift_time(chime, _peak_time(dsa) - _peak_time(chime)), dsa]

=== toa_offset_at_dm_ms (what offset does it feed _align_toa?) ===
def toa_offset_at_dm_ms(
    nick: str,
    target_dm: float,
    *,
    toa_results: Path = TOA_RESULTS,
    toa_fixture: Path = TOA_FIXTURE,
) -> float | None:
    """Re-reference the legacy CHIME-minus-DSA 400-MHz offset to ``target_dm``.

    The tracked CHIME TOA is already expressed at 400 MHz. The DSA TOA was
    propagated from the fixture's native frequency to 400 MHz using the
    legacy row DM, so changing that DM by ``delta_dm`` changes the DSA TOA by
    ``K_DM * delta_dm * (400^-2 - nu_DSA^-2)``. Because the stored offset is
    CHIME minus DSA, that correction is subtracted from the legacy offset.
    """
    file_nick = FILE_NICK.get(nick, nick)
    rows = json.loads(toa_results.read_text())
    row = rows.get(file_nick) or rows.get(file_nick.lower())
    if row is None:
        return None
    fixture = json.loads(toa_fixture.read_text())
    f_ref = float(fixture["reference_frequency_mhz"])
    f_dsa = float(fixture["dsa_native_frequency_mhz"])
    delta_dm = float(target_dm) - float(row["dm"])
    dsa_delta_ms = (
        1e3 * K_DM_S_MHZ2 * delta_dm * (f_ref**-2 - f_dsa**-2)
    )
    return float(row["measured_offset_ms"]) - dsa_delta_ms


def _toa_offset(nick: str, *, target_dm: float | None = None) -> float | None:
    """Measured CHIME-minus-DSA offset in the requested DM convention.

    Crossmatch rows use file nicknames (for example ``johndoeII``), so map
    manuscript nicknames through ``FILE_NICK`` before either lookup.
    """
    file_nick = FILE_NICK.get(nick, nick)
    if target_dm is None:
        return toa_offset_ms(file_nick)
    return toa_offset_at_dm_ms(file_nick, target_dm)

"""Render Figure 1: a 12-panel grid of the joint CHIME/DSA observations.

Every panel is drawn from the archival `_cntr_bpc.npy` products at near-native
display resolution (1024 channels per band; DSA-110 native 32.768 us time
sampling, CHIME/FRB block-averaged to ~33.3 us to match), NOT from the coarse
fit-delivery grids stored in the jointmodel NPZs — those are for the model
audit (triptychs), where data and model must share one grid. Both archival
products span the full ~82 ms burst-centered window, so the shared
CHIME-width display crop never runs off the end of either band.

Each cell carries the band-summed profile strip on top and the time-integrated
on-pulse spectrum marginal on the right; RFI-excised (zapped/flat) channels are
NaN-masked and render in a uniform gray in every panel. No model or residual
values are drawn. Before display averaging, both native-resolution products
are re-dedispersed from their filename-stem DMs to the adopted CHIME
phase-coherence DM in ``analysis/dm-joint-phase-v2/manuscript_dm_catalog.csv``.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import csv
import json
import warnings
from pathlib import Path

import matplotlib

matplotlib.use("Agg")
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np
from matplotlib.patches import Rectangle

from plot_codetection_gallery import _apply_style, onpulse_span
from plot_codetection_triptych import (
    DATA_ROOT_DEFAULT,
    MANIFEST_DEFAULT,
    ROOT,
    bands_archival,
    load_manifest,
)

OUT_DEFAULT = ROOT / "figures" / "codetection_data_grid"
DM_CATALOG_DEFAULT = ROOT / "analysis" / "dm-joint-phase-v2" / "manuscript_dm_catalog.csv"

# Block-averaging factors of the native archival grids (f_factor, t_factor):
# DSA 6144ch/32.768us -> 512ch at native time (1024ch buries the faintest DSA
# detections below per-pixel noise); CHIME 1024ch/2.56us -> native channels at
# ~33.3us, matching the DSA time resolution across the gap.
DISPLAY_FACTORS = {"dsa": (12, 1), "chime": (1, 13)}

# Display padding around the on-pulse union, as a fraction of the CHIME
# on-pulse width per side (1.5 ms floor). Half a CHIME width keeps the burst
# filling the panel instead of sitting in off-pulse noise; the absolute cap
# stops heavily scattered bursts (CHIME width tens of ms) from inflating the

"""Compute per-band Delta_scat (peak-shift, band-center, flux-weighted) from the
FIGURE's own jointmodel NPZ + fit JSON, identical method to the ToA analysis."""
import sys, json, warnings
from pathlib import Path
import numpy as np
sys.path.insert(0,"/Users/jakobfaber/Developer/repos/github.com/jakobtfaber/dsa110-FLITS")
sys.path.insert(0,"/Users/jakobfaber/Developer/repos/github.com/jakobtfaber/dsa110-FLITS/scattering")
from scat_analysis.burstfit import (FRBModel, BETA_THIN_SCREEN_MIN, BETA_THIN_SCREEN_MAX,
    BETA_EXP_EPS, analytic_gaussian_exp_convolution as _agec,
    gaussian_powerlaw_convolution as _gpc)

NATIVE_DF={"chime":0.390625,"dsa":0.03051757812}

def _peak_shift(sig,tau,beta):
    sig=float(max(sig,1e-6)); tau=float(max(tau,1e-6))
    span=max(20*tau,12*sig,2.0); dt=min(sig,tau)/50.0; dt=max(dt,span/400000)
    tg=np.arange(-span,span,dt); mu=np.array([[0.0]]); S=np.array([[sig]]); T=np.array([[tau]])
    bpbf=float(np.clip(beta,BETA_THIN_SCREEN_MIN,BETA_THIN_SCREEN_MAX))
    y=(_agec(tg,mu,S,T)[0] if bpbf>=BETA_THIN_SCREEN_MAX-BETA_EXP_EPS else _gpc(tg,mu,S,T,bpbf)[0])
    i=int(np.argmax(y))
    if 0<i<len(y)-1:
        d=y[i-1]-2*y[i]+y[i+1]; off=0.5*(y[i-1]-y[i+1])/d if d!=0 else 0.0
    else: off=0.0
    return float(tg[i]+off*dt)

def scatter_shift_ms(npz_path, dm_init_map):
    """Return {'CHIME/FRB': Dscat_C_ms, 'DSA-110': Dscat_D_ms} for a burst's NPZ."""
    fitj=Path(str(npz_path).replace("_jointmodel_","_joint_fit_")).with_suffix(".json")
    perc=json.loads(fitj.read_text())["percentiles"]
    p={k:v["median"] for k,v in perc.items()}
    z=np.load(npz_path,allow_pickle=True)
    tau=float(z["tau_1ghz"]); beta=float(z["beta"]); alpha=float(z["alpha"])
    out={}
    for band,tel,label in (("C","chime","CHIME/FRB"),("D","dsa","DSA-110")):
        t=np.asarray(z[f"time{band}"],float); freq=np.asarray(z[f"freq{band}"],float)
        npzm=np.asarray(z[f"model{band}"],float); data=np.asarray(z[f"data{band}"],float)
        noise=z[f"noise{band}"] if f"noise{band}" in z.files else None
        prof_m=np.nansum(npzm,0); use=(prof_m.max()-prof_m.min())/(abs(prof_m.max())+1e-30)>=0.05
        src=npzm if use else data; prof=np.nansum(src,0); Ppk=int(np.argmax(prof))
        m=FRBModel(t,freq,data=npzm,dm_init=dm_init_map[tel],df_MHz=NATIVE_DF[tel],beta=beta,noise_std=noise)
        # dominant component params: t0 nearest collapsed peak
        t0keys=sorted([k for k in p if k.startswith(f"t0_{band}")])
        t0s={k:p[k] for k in t0keys}
        Ppk_ms=float(t[Ppk])
        dom=min(t0s,key=lambda k:abs(t0s[k]-Ppk_ms))
        # component index for zeta/delta_dm
        suf=dom[len(f"t0_{band}"):]
        zeta=p.get(f"zeta_{band}{suf}", p.get(f"zeta_{band}", 0.0))
        ddm=p.get(f"delta_dm_{band}", 0.0)
        tau_f=np.clip(tau*(freq/1.0)**(-alpha),1e-6,None)
        sig_f=np.atleast_1d(m._smearing_sigma(m.dm_init+ddm,zeta)).astype(float)
        if sig_f.size==1: sig_f=np.full(len(freq),float(sig_f))
        sig_f=np.clip(sig_f,1e-6,None)
        chflux=np.clip(np.nansum(src,1),0,None); ok=chflux>0
        ps=np.array([_peak_shift(sig_f[fi],tau_f[fi],beta) for fi in range(len(freq))])
        Dscat=float(np.sum(chflux[ok]*ps[ok])/np.sum(chflux[ok]))
        out[label]=Dscat
    z.close()
    return out
PYEOF

# Rigid-band model: each band has peak and arrival Δ_scat apart (arrival EARLIER).
# bands_archival gives each band t=0 at its own DATA peak; _align_toa shifts ONLY
# CHIME so CHIME peak lands at O_peak (the peak offset), DSA peak stays at 0.
# QUESTION: what per-band extra_shift makes the DISPLAY match the ToA analysis?
import numpy as np
# oran example
O_peak=6.1938; Dc=3.1393; Dd=0.1087
diff=Dc-Dd; O_model=O_peak-diff
# After _align_toa(O_peak): DSA peak 0, CHIME peak O_peak.
# arrivals currently: DSA = -Dd, CHIME = O_peak - Dc
print("baseline (peak-aligned) arrivals: DSA=%.4f CHIME=%.4f  arrival_sep=%.4f (=O_model? %.4f)"%(
    -Dd, O_peak-Dc, (O_peak-Dc)-(-Dd), O_model))
# So arrivals are ALREADY separated by O_model with pure peak alignment.
# The current code instead applies band-TOP-t0 shifts that DIFFER per band -> corrupts sep.
# Correct arrival-anchored placement: DSA arrival->0, CHIME arrival->O_model
sD = 0-(-Dd)              # +Dd
sC = O_model-(O_peak-Dc)  # = -diff+Dc = Dd
print("correct extra_shift: DSA=%.4f CHIME=%.4f  (differential=%.4f -> global shift)"%(sD,sC,sC-sD))
# verify final
dsa_peak=0+sD; chime_peak=O_peak+sC
print("final: DSA peak=%.4f arrival=%.4f | CHIME peak=%.4f arrival=%.4f"%(
    dsa_peak, dsa_peak-Dd, chime_peak, chime_peak-Dc))
print("final arrival_sep=%.4f (O_model=%.4f) | peak_sep=%.4f (O_peak=%.4f)"%(
    (chime_peak-Dc)-(dsa_peak-Dd), O_model, chime_peak-dsa_peak, O_peak))

import sys, json; sys.path.insert(0,"scripts")
import numpy as np
from pathlib import Path
import plot_codetection_triptych as T
import plot_codetection_data_grid as g
DATA=Path("/Users/jakobfaber/Data/Faber2026/dsa110/DSA_bursts")
adm=g.load_adopted_dms(g.DM_CATALOG_DEFAULT)
rows={r["nick"]:r for r in g.load_manifest(g.MANIFEST_DEFAULT)}
JSON=json.load(open("pipeline/crossmatching/toa_crossmatch_results.json"))

def published_dscat(nick):
    row=JSON.get(nick) or JSON.get(nick.lower()) or next((JSON[k] for k in JSON if k.lower()==nick.lower()),None)
    if not row or "scatter_corr_chime_ms" not in row: return None
    return row["scatter_corr_chime_ms"], row["scatter_corr_dsa_ms"]

def peaks(bands):
    o={}
    for b in bands:
        t=np.asarray(b.time_ms,float); prof=np.nansum(np.asarray(b.data,float),0)
        o[b.label]=float(t[int(np.nanargmax(prof))])
    return o

print(f"{'burst':11s}{'Opk@dm':>8s}{'Dc':>7s}{'Dd':>7s}{'pkSEP_new':>10s}{'arrSEP_new':>11s}{'Omodel@dm':>10s}")
for nick in ["casey","oran","isha","phineas","chromatica","zach"]:
    r=rows[nick]; tdm=adm[nick]
    dd=published_dscat(nick)
    Opk=T._toa_offset(nick,target_dm=tdm)
    if dd is None or Opk is None:
        # chromatica: npz null -> peak anchor, no shift
        b=T.bands_archival(DATA,nick,factors=g.DISPLAY_FACTORS,pad_scale=g.DISPLAY_PAD_SCALE,
            pad_cap_ms=g.DISPLAY_PAD_CAP_MS,target_dm=tdm,extra_shift_ms={})
        p=peaks(b); print(f"{nick:11s}{(Opk or float('nan')):8.3f}   (peak anchor / no npz) pkSEP={p['CHIME/FRB']-p['DSA-110']:.3f}")
        continue
    Dc,Dd=dd; diff=Dc-Dd
    # arrival-anchored global shift +Dd both bands
    extra={"CHIME/FRB":Dd, "DSA-110":Dd}
    b=T.bands_archival(DATA,nick,factors=g.DISPLAY_FACTORS,pad_scale=g.DISPLAY_PAD_SCALE,
        pad_cap_ms=g.DISPLAY_PAD_CAP_MS,target_dm=tdm,extra_shift_ms=extra)
    p=peaks(b); pkSEP=p['CHIME/FRB']-p['DSA-110']; arrSEP=pkSEP-diff
    print(f"{nick:11s}{Opk:8.3f}{Dc:7.3f}{Dd:7.3f}{pkSEP:10.4f}{arrSEP:11.4f}{Opk-diff:10.4f}")

+def _published_scatter_corr(nick: str) -> tuple[float, float] | None:
+    """Adopted per-band scattering correction (Delta_scat, ms) for a burst.
+
+    Reads the published values ``scatter_corr_chime_ms`` / ``scatter_corr_dsa_ms``
+    from the crossmatch results tracked in the pipeline submodule. These are the
+    band-averaged, flux-weighted peak shifts of the fitted scattering kernel
+    (intrinsic model arrival = observed profile peak - Delta_scat), computed in
+    the ToA analysis; using them here keeps the figure identical to that analysis
+    rather than re-deriving from the figure's display NPZs (a different, DM-locked
+    fit family whose Delta_scat diverges from the published free-DM values).
+
+    Returns ``None`` when no correction is tracked (e.g. chromatica, no accepted
+    joint fit): the burst then keeps the observed-peak anchor.
+    """
+    from plot_codetection_triptych import FILE_NICK, TOA_RESULTS
+
+    file_nick = FILE_NICK.get(nick, nick)
+    rows = json.loads(Path(TOA_RESULTS).read_text())
+    row = rows.get(file_nick) or rows.get(file_nick.lower())
+    if row is None:
+        row = next((v for k, v in rows.items() if k.lower() == file_nick.lower()), None)
+    if row is None or "scatter_corr_chime_ms" not in row:
+        return None
+    return float(row["scatter_corr_chime_ms"]), float(row["scatter_corr_dsa_ms"])
+
+
+def fit_toa_shift_ms(
+    row: dict, *, root: Path, data_root: Path, target_dm: float
+) -> dict[str, float]:
+    """Per-band shift (band label -> ms) anchoring each band on its adopted
+    intrinsic model arrival rather than on the observed (scattered) profile peak.
+
+    The ToA analysis defines the arrival time as the joint-fit model t0 with the
+    scattering tail deconvolved out: intrinsic arrival = observed peak minus the
+    band-averaged scattering peak shift Delta_scat (see ``_published_scatter_corr``).
+    The scattered peak lags the arrival, and by more at CHIME (0.6 GHz) than at
+    DSA (1.4 GHz) because tau ~ nu^-alpha; the earlier convention (dominant-component
+    t0 referenced to the top of each band) carried a dispersion mismatch and did not
+    cancel this differential, so the displayed CHIME-DSA separation disagreed with the
+    ToA analysis (most severely for oran: 3.4 ms off, sign flipped).
+
+    ``bands_archival`` first calls ``_align_toa`` with the measured CHIME-minus-DSA
+    *peak* offset, which places the two observed peaks at that separation. Adding the
+    same DSA scattering correction to both bands is a pure translation that moves the
+    DSA intrinsic arrival to t=0; the CHIME intrinsic arrival then lands at
+    peak_offset - (Delta_scat_C - Delta_scat_D) = the model-corrected offset. The
+    observed peaks stay separated by the measured peak offset (the scattering tail
+    remains visible); the intrinsic arrivals are separated by the model offset,
+    matching the ToA analysis. Everything is expressed at the adopted display DM,
+    consistent with the re-dedispersion applied to the waterfalls.
+
+    Empty for rows without a tracked scattering correction (observed-peak anchor kept).
+    """
+    corr = _published_scatter_corr(row["nick"])
+    if corr is None:
+        return {}
+    _scatter_corr_chime_ms, scatter_corr_dsa_ms = corr
+    return {"CHIME/FRB": scatter_corr_dsa_ms, "DSA-110": scatter_corr_dsa_ms}

--- README: what triggers re-review ---
# Figure candidate review and approval

Scientific figures use a two-PR, fail-closed workflow. Automated tests and an
agent's visual inspection can establish that a figure is reproducible and
legible; they do **not** constitute manuscript-owner approval.

## 1. Candidate PR

Generate figures outside their manuscript targets, then stage an immutable
review batch:

```bash
python scripts/figure_review.py new-batch 2026-07-14-example \
  --title "Example candidate batch" \
  --candidate-root /path/to/isolated/render-output \
  --pipeline-revision "<exact FLITS commit>"
```

The candidate root mirrors manuscript-relative output paths (for example,
`/path/to/isolated/render-output/figures/codetection_data_grid.pdf`) so staging
never writes into live manuscript targets. The command copies those PDFs into
`figure_review/batches/<batch>/`, records
their SHA-256 values and the adopted-DM catalog SHA-256, renders first-page PNG
previews, and builds `index.html`. A candidate PR contains only this review
packet and any generator/provenance changes. It does not edit TeX inclusions or
promote files into `figures/`.

Review by stable candidate ID. Record each owner decision separately:

```bash
python scripts/figure_review.py decide <batch> joint-oran approved \
  --reviewer "Jakob Faber" --note "DM, residuals, labels, and fit accepted"

python scripts/figure_review.py decide <batch> dsa-acf-zach needs_revision \
  --reviewer "Jakob Faber" --note "Broader component is assigned incorrectly"
```

Silence, automated checks, PR creation, and agent review never imply approval.

## 2. Promotion PR

import json
m=json.load(open("figure_review/batches/2026-07-17-fig1-model-toa/manifest.json"))

    for v in m.values():
        if isinstance(v,list) and v and isinstance(v[0],dict) and "artifact_sha256" in v[0]: recs=v;break

# spot check a triptych + fig5 unchanged vs their current tracked bytes
import hashlib
from pathlib import Path
def sha(p): return hashlib.sha256(Path(p).read_bytes()).hexdigest()
for sid in ["fig5-association","triptych-oran","joint-oran"]:

import json, numpy as np
from astropy.coordinates import SkyCoord, EarthLocation
from astropy.time import Time
import astropy.units as u

fx=json.load(open("crossmatching/notebook_reproduction_fixture.json"))
res=json.load(open("crossmatching/toa_crossmatch_results.json"))
DRAO=EarthLocation(lat=49.3206*u.deg, lon=-119.6236*u.deg, height=545*u.m)  # CHIME
OVRO=EarthLocation(lat=37.2333*u.deg, lon=-118.2834*u.deg, height=1222*u.m) # DSA
rows={r["name"]:r for r in fx["bursts"]}

print(f"{'burst':11s}{'RA':>8s}{'Dec':>8s}{'HA_chime_deg':>13s}{'model_off':>11s}{'geom':>8s}{'resid':>8s}{'who_first':>11s}")
data=[]
for name,r in rows.items():
    c=SkyCoord(r["source_coord"], unit=(u.hourangle,u.deg))
    # CHIME toa (unix) -> Time
    t=Time(r["chime"]["toa_unix_400"], format="unix")
    lst=t.sidereal_time("apparent", longitude=DRAO.lon)
    ha=(lst - c.ra).wrap_at(180*u.deg).deg
    rr=res[name]
    off=rr["measured_offset_ms"]; geo=rr["geometric_delay_ms"]
    resid=off-geo
    first="DSA" if off>0 else "CHIME"   # measured_offset = chime_toa - dsa_toa; >0 => chime later => DSA first
    data.append((name,c.ra.deg,c.dec.deg,ha,off,geo,resid,first))
    print(f"{name:11s}{c.ra.deg:8.2f}{c.dec.deg:8.2f}{ha:13.2f}{off:11.3f}{geo:8.3f}{resid:8.3f}{first:>11s}")

decs=[d[2] for d in data]; has=[d[3] for d in data]; geos=[d[5] for d in data]
print()
print(f"Dec range: {min(decs):.1f} to {max(decs):.1f} deg (spread {max(decs)-min(decs):.1f})")
print(f"HA range:  {min(has):.1f} to {max(has):.1f} deg")
print(f"geom_delay range: {min(geos):.3f} to {max(geos):.3f} ms (spread {max(geos)-min(geos):.3f})")

import numpy as np
# The measured_offset is defined = chime_toa_400 - dsa_toa_400 (both referred to 400 MHz
# with the SAME shared DM). geometric_delay is the CHIME-minus-DSA arrival-time delay
# from the baseline projection. So:
#   measured_offset = geometric_delay + intrinsic_offset + dm_referral_mismatch
# Under a perfectly shared DM and identical intrinsic emission time across bands,
# residual would be ~0. The residual's SIGN and MAGNITUDE therefore encode the
# combination of (a) any DM error (band-dependent 400-MHz referral) and
# (b) genuine intrinsic band-to-band emission-time differences.
# Show DM-referral sensitivity: d(offset)/dDM = -9.408 ms per pc/cm3 (shared-DM).
# So a residual of e.g. -3.49 ms (chromatica) vs +6.67 (wilhelm) spanning ~10 ms
# corresponds to an equivalent DM swing of ~1 pc/cm3 IF it were purely DM.
K=4148.808; fref=400.0; fdsa=1405.0; fchime=600.39
dodDM = K*(1/fchime**2 - 1/fref**2) - K*(1/fdsa**2 - 1/fref**2)
print("d(offset)/dDM shared =", round(dodDM,4), "ms per pc/cm3")
resid=[1.350,2.148,5.378,-0.238,6.671,2.787,3.748,4.332,1.192,-0.957,-3.486,-0.425]
print("residual span:", round(max(resid)-min(resid),2), "ms")
print("equiv DM span if purely DM:", round((max(resid)-min(resid))/abs(dodDM),3), "pc/cm3")
print("residual mean/std:", round(np.mean(resid),3), round(np.std(resid),3))

# offset = toa400_chime - toa400_dsa ; DM part = K*DM*(1/f_dsa^2 - 1/f_chime^2)

import json, numpy as np, matplotlib as mpl, matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import csv
apply_figure_style(sizes=(8,7,6))

rows=list(csv.DictReader(open("/Users/jakobfaber/handoffs/toa_model/offset_decomposition.csv")))
for r in rows:
    for k in ("model_offset_ms","geometric_delay_ms","residual_ms","equiv_dDM"):
        r[k]=float(r[k])
rows.sort(key=lambda x:x["model_offset_ms"])
names=[r["burst"] for r in rows]
off=np.array([r["model_offset_ms"] for r in rows])
geo=np.array([r["geometric_delay_ms"] for r in rows])
resid=np.array([r["residual_ms"] for r in rows])
y=np.arange(len(rows))[::-1]   # top = most negative? we'll invert so DSA-first at top

# geometric cluster band
gmin,gmax=geo.min(),geo.max()

pal=focal_palette(["offset","geometry"], focal="offset", focal_color="#1f77b4")
c_off, c_geo = pal[0], pal[1]

fig,ax=plt.subplots(figsize=(7.2,5.4))
# geometric pedestal band
ax.axvspan(gmin,gmax,color=c_geo,alpha=0.18,zorder=0,lw=0)
ax.axvline(0,color="0.35",lw=1.0,zorder=1)
# residual connector: from geometry to total offset
for yi,g,o in zip(y,geo,off):
    ax.plot([g,o],[yi,yi],color="0.6",lw=1.4,zorder=2,solid_capstyle="round")
# geometry markers (all clustered)
ax.scatter(geo,y,marker="|",s=140,color=c_geo,zorder=3,lw=2.0)
# total model offset markers
ax.scatter(off,y,s=46,color=c_off,zorder=4,edgecolor="white",lw=0.6)
ax.set_yticks(y); ax.set_yticklabels(names)
ax.set_xlabel("CHIME $-$ DSA arrival offset at 400 MHz (ms)")
ax.margins(y=0.03)
# who-first annotations at top
ax.text(0.02,1.02,"CHIME arrives first",transform=ax.transAxes,ha="left",va="bottom",color="0.25",fontsize=7)
ax.text(0.98,1.02,"DSA arrives first",transform=ax.transAxes,ha="right",va="bottom",color="0.25",fontsize=7)

# legend via direct proxies
from matplotlib.lines import Line2D
from matplotlib.patches import Patch
leg=[Line2D([0],[0],marker="|",color=c_geo,lw=0,markersize=10,markeredgewidth=2,label="geometric delay (baseline)"),
     Line2D([0],[0],marker="o",color=c_off,lw=0,markersize=6,markeredgecolor="white",label="measured model offset"),
     Patch(facecolor=c_geo,alpha=0.18,label="geometric range (all 12)")]
ax.legend(handles=leg,loc="lower right",frameon=False,fontsize=6.5)

# secondary top axis: equivalent shared-DM error
DODDM=-9.4078
secax=ax.secondary_xaxis("top",functions=(lambda x:x/DODDM, lambda d:d*DODDM))
secax.set_xlabel(r"equivalent shared-DM offset (pc cm$^{-3}$)")

fig.tight_layout()
fig.savefig("toa_offset_decomposition.png",dpi=300,bbox_inches="tight")
print("gmin,gmax:",round(gmin,3),round(gmax,3))
print("saved")
stdout:
gmin,gmax: -2.297 -2.034
saved

stdout:
=== P_cc chance-coincidence section in toa.tex ===
152: (Section~\ref{sec:toa-pcc}) uses the full folded error as its coincidence
153: tolerance.
154: 
155: \subsubsection{Chance-coincidence probability}
156: \label{sec:toa-pcc}
157: 
158: A small timing residual shows that a CHIME and a DSA trigger are \emph{consistent}
159: with being the same burst; it does not by itself show that they \emph{are}. The
160: complementary question is how often an \emph{unrelated} CHIME burst would fall
161: close enough to a given DSA burst---in arrival time, sky position, and dispersion
162: measure simultaneously---to mimic the association by chance. We quantify this with
163: a chance-coincidence (false-alarm) probability $P_{\rm cc}$.
164: 
165: We model unrelated CHIME bursts as a Poisson point process on the sky. For DSA
166: burst $i$, the expected number of unrelated CHIME bursts that land inside the joint
167: association window is
168: \begin{equation}
169: \mu_i = R \,\frac{\Omega_{\rm win}}{\Omega_{\rm sky}}\, (2\,\Delta t)\, f_{\rm DM}(\mathrm{DM}_i,\Delta\mathrm{DM}),
170: \label{eq:pcc_mu}
171: \end{equation}
172: where $R$ is the all-sky CHIME burst rate (in
173: $\mathrm{bursts\;sky^{-1}\,day^{-1}}$),
174: $\Omega_{\rm win}$ is the sky area of the positional window and
175: $\Omega_{\rm sky} = 4\pi\,\mathrm{sr} \simeq 41{,}253\,\mathrm{deg^2}$ the full
176: sky---so $\Omega_{\rm win}/\Omega_{\rm sky}$ is the fraction of the sky covered
177: by the positional gate---$2\,\Delta t$ is the
178: full arrival-time window (a $\pm\Delta t$ coincidence gate, expressed in days), and
179: $f_{\rm DM}(\mathrm{DM}_i,\Delta\mathrm{DM})$ is the fraction of the CHIME DM
180: distribution lying within $\pm\Delta\mathrm{DM}$ of the burst's DM---the factor
181: that additionally requires a chance interloper to match in DM, not only in
182: time and position. For such a rare-event Poisson process the probability that
183: \emph{at least one} unrelated burst falls in the window is
184: \begin{equation}
185: P_{{\rm cc},i} = 1 - e^{-\mu_i} \simeq \mu_i \qquad (\mu_i \ll 1),
186: \label{eq:pcc_p}
187: \end{equation}
188: so $P_{\rm cc}$ is, to excellent approximation, just the expected number of chance
189: matches.
190: 
191: Equation~\eqref{eq:pcc_mu} conditions on a single DSA burst, so the sample-level
192: false-alarm expectation is the sum $\sum_j \mu_j$ over the \emph{trial set}: the
193: complete list of DSA-110 triggers searched for a CHIME/FRB counterpart over the
194: 2022~February--2024~February overlap, not merely the twelve that matched. The
195: look-elsewhere penalty is therefore carried explicitly by that sum. Under the
196: chance-maximizing windows below, the eight pairs whose pre-specified
197: association statistic included a DM constraint have
198: $\mu_j\sim2$--$6\times10^{-9}$, while the four position-and-time-only pairs use
199: $f_{\rm DM}=1$ and have $\mu_j=4.4\times10^{-7}$. The twelve reported pairs sum
200: to $\sum_i\mu_i\approx1.8\times10^{-6}$. Even the conservative extension to the
201: entire 64-burst DSA-110 trial set, obtained by scaling by the searched-to-matched
202: ratio $64/12$, remains below $10^{-5}$ and leaves the conclusion unchanged.
203: 
204: We evaluate Eq.~\eqref{eq:pcc_mu} with deliberately conservative,
205: chance-\emph{maximizing} inputs, so the reported $P_{\rm cc}$ is an upper bound on
206: the true false-alarm rate: an all-sky CHIME rate rounded up to
207: $R = 10^{3}\,\mathrm{sky^{-1}\,day^{-1}}$ (above the Catalog~1 value of
208: $\simeq525\,\mathrm{sky^{-1}\,day^{-1}}$; \citealt{CHIME2021Catalog}), a
209: $0.5^{\circ}$-radius positional disk ($\Omega_{\rm win}=0.785\,\mathrm{deg^2}$,
210: i.e.\ $\Omega_{\rm win}/\Omega_{\rm sky}=1.90\times10^{-5}$),
211: $\Delta t = \pm1\,\mathrm{s}$ ($2\,\Delta t = 2.31\times10^{-5}$\,day), and
212: $\Delta\mathrm{DM}=\pm5\,\mathrm{pc\,cm^{-3}}$. With these inputs and
213: $f_{\rm DM}=1$, Eq.~\eqref{eq:pcc_mu} evaluates directly to
214: $\mu = 10^{3}\times1.90\times10^{-5}\times2.31\times10^{-5}
215: = 4.4\times10^{-7}$, the position-and-time-only value quoted below and in
216: Table~\ref{tab:sample}.
217: These windows are far wider than the actual per-event tolerances (millisecond
218: timing, arcsecond-scale DSA localization, and the measured DM agreement), so using
219: each event's true tolerances can only lower $P_{\rm cc}$. As an independent check,
220: a direct Monte-Carlo of the background point process reproduces the closed-form
221: $P_{\rm cc}$ to $0.3\%$ in a regime where both are measurable, and the result is

import json, math, sys
sys.path.insert(0,".")
from crossmatching.association import residual_pedestal
d=json.load(open("crossmatching/toa_crossmatch_results.json"))
# residual = offset - geometric_delay ; use combined_error_full as the per-point error
resid=[]; err=[]; geo=[]
for b,r in d.items():
    resid.append(r["measured_offset_ms"]-r["geometric_delay_ms"])
    err.append(r["combined_error_full_ms"])
    geo.append(r["geometric_delay_ms"])
# 1) test the geometric pedestal itself: is mean geometric delay significant & consistent?
import statistics as st
print("geometric delay: mean %.3f ms, spread %.3f ms (min %.3f max %.3f)"%(
    st.mean(geo), max(geo)-min(geo), min(geo), max(geo)))
# 2) residual pedestal significance (does the residual have a significant nonzero mean?)
rp=residual_pedestal(resid,err)
print("residual pedestal: weighted-mean %.3f +/- %.3f ms  => %.2f sigma"%(
    rp["weighted_mean_ms"],rp["error_ms"],rp["n_sigma"]))
# 3) key association point: does any offset EXCEED the conservative +/-1s timing gate? 
maxoff=max(abs(r["measured_offset_ms"]) for r in d.values())
print("max |offset| = %.3f ms vs conservative gate 1000 ms => %.2e of gate"%(maxoff, maxoff/1000))
PYEOF

# --- Assemble the association report (golden artifact never touched) -----------
def _load_chime_inputs(chime_inputs_path) -> dict:
    """Index CHIME-side extraction rows by chime_id (empty dict if path absent/None)."""
    if not chime_inputs_path or not Path(chime_inputs_path).exists():
        return {}
    rows = json.loads(Path(chime_inputs_path).read_text())
    return {str(r["chime_id"]): r for r in rows if "dm_chime" in r}


def build_association_report(
    fixture_path,
    *,
    rate_per_day: float = 1000.0,
    omega_win_deg2: float = OMEGA_WIN_BASELINE_DEG2,
    dt_s: float = DT_BASELINE_S,
    ddm: float = DDM_BASELINE,
    chime_inputs_path=None,
    chime_radius_deg: float = 0.1,
) -> dict:
    """Run all four pillars over the fixture and return the report dict. Read-only on disk.

    Pillars 2 (CHIME DM) and 4 (CHIME position) activate per burst when a CHIME-side inputs
    file is supplied (``chime_inputs_path``, keyed by chime_id); bursts absent from it emit
    explicit null+reason rather than fabricated values.
    """
    fx = json.loads(Path(fixture_path).read_text())
    chime = _load_chime_inputs(chime_inputs_path)
    bursts, dms = [], []
    for row in fx["bursts"]:
        dm = row["dm"]
        dms.append(dm)
        ci = chime.get(str(row["chime_id"]), {})
        bursts.append(
            {
                "name": row["name"],
                "chime_id": row["chime_id"],
                "dm": dm,
                "chance_coincidence_P": chance_probability(
                    dm, rate_per_day=rate_per_day, omega_win_deg2=omega_win_deg2, dt_s=dt_s, ddm=ddm
                ),
                "dm_agreement": dm_agreement(
                    dm_chime=ci.get("dm_chime"),
                    dm_chime_err=ci.get("dm_chime_err"),
                    dm_dsa=dm,
                    dm_dsa_err=row.get("dm_uncertainty"),
                ),
                "dm_confidence": ci.get("dm_confidence"),  # figure-review: real/marginal/noise
                "position": position_agreement(
                    row.get("source_coord"),
                    ci.get("chime_ra_deg"),
                    ci.get("chime_dec_deg"),
                    chime_radius_deg,
                ),
            }
        )
    return {
        "inputs": {
            "rate_per_day": rate_per_day,
            "omega_win_deg2": omega_win_deg2,
            "dt_s": dt_s,
            "ddm": ddm,
            "dm_model": "lognormal(500,0.7) [assumption]",
            "chime_dm_method": "arrival-time regression (scatter-deconvolved EMG sub-band t0 vs "
            "nu^-2; coherent_dedisp at DSA DM; uniform TDS=32/N_SB=6) on library coherent_dedisp; "
            "8/12 bursts constrained, 4 unconstrained (<3 sub-bands above S/N 4). Supersedes the "
            "retracted DM-phase extraction (1e-3*K_DM inter-channel unit bug). Per-burst DMs and "
            "CHIME-DSA agreement in dm_provenance.csv; see .agents/audit-chime-side-dm.md.",
            "chime_localization_radius_deg": chime_radius_deg,
            "chime_localization_note": "tiedbeam pointing; no multi-beam error ellipse "
            "(Michilli+2021 sub-arcmin assumed)",
        },
        "expected_chance_associations": expected_chance_associations(
            dms, rate_per_day=rate_per_day, omega_win_deg2=omega_win_deg2, dt_s=dt_s, ddm=ddm
        ),
        "bursts": bursts,
    }


def main() -> None:
    here = Path(__file__).resolve().parent

stdout:
timing, arcsecond-scale DSA localization, and the measured DM agreement), so using
each event's true tolerances can only lower $P_{\rm cc}$. As an independent check,
a direct Monte-Carlo of the background point process reproduces the closed-form
$P_{\rm cc}$ to $0.3\%$ in a regime where both are measurable, and the result is
stable across several orders of magnitude of variation in each window and in $R$.

Two idealizations of the Poisson background deserve explicit sensitivity
statements. First, the model takes $R$ as an isotropic all-sky rate, whereas
the sample sits in a narrow high-declination band ($+70^{\circ}$ to
$+74^{\circ}$) where CHIME's per-solid-angle exposure---and hence its detected
burst surface density---exceeds the all-sky mean: the transit dwell time
scales as $1/\cos\delta\simeq3.2$ at $\delta=72^{\circ}$, and these
declinations also receive lower-transit coverage. The adopted
$R=10^{3}\,\mathrm{sky^{-1}\,day^{-1}}$ already doubles the Catalog~1 rate;
even inflating the \emph{local} rate density by a further factor of ten
raises the sample-summed expectation only to
$\sum_i\mu_i\approx1.8\times10^{-5}$ (and the conservative 64-trigger
extension to $\lesssim10^{-4}$), leaving the association verdict unchanged.

sed -n '236,275p' sections/toa.tex
stdout:
Second, the model assumes non-repeating background sources, while repeaters
make close-time coincidences more likely than Poisson within the $\pm1$\,s
gate. The DSA-110 detection list over the overlap window includes two bursts
of the known repeater FRB~20220912A, and none of the twelve co-detected
counterparts has a repeat detection in either dataset; because repetition
enters Eq.~\eqref{eq:pcc_mu} only through the effective CHIME rate inside the
$\pm1$\,s gate, even a repeater-driven rate enhancement of $10^{3}$ leaves
$\sum_i\mu_i\lesssim10^{-2}$.

\subsubsection{Residuals and systematics}

Table~\ref{tab:sample} summarizes the association diagnostics. The tabulated
residuals use the shared DSA-DM convention: the CHIME and DSA arrivals are both
referenced to 400\,MHz with the DSA catalog DM, so the residual is a
timing/geometry consistency check rather than an independent per-telescope-DM
comparison. Re-referencing the CHIME arrivals with their independently measured
DMs would inject millisecond-scale shifts for the largest CHIME--DSA DM offsets,
so those shifts are tracked separately rather than folded into the association
residual.

We adopt the sign convention $\Delta t = \Delta t_{\rm inter\text{-}site} -
\tau_{\rm geo}$, where $\Delta t_{\rm inter\text{-}site}$ is the CHIME-minus-DSA
arrival offset after both are referenced to 400\,MHz (Eq.~\ref{eq:dmdelay}) and
$\tau_{\rm geo}$ is the predicted geometric delay (Eq.~\ref{eq:geodelay}); a
positive residual means the CHIME arrival is later than the shared-DM geometric
prediction relative to DSA, and $\Delta t=0$ is exact agreement. The geometric
term is itself bounded: for the $1345\,\mathrm{km}$ CHIME (DRAO)--DSA-110 (OVRO)
baseline, $|\tau_{\rm geo}| \le |\mathbf{p}_2-\mathbf{p}_1|/c \approx 4.5\,$ms for
any source direction. For the twelve source directions here,
$\tau_{\rm geo}=-2.30$ to $-2.03\,$ms: the wavefront reaches CHIME first and
DSA-110 $2.03$--$2.30\,$ms later. The residual is therefore not error-free but carries a
per-burst systematic of order the inter-site clock/timestamp alignment
($\sigma_{\rm clock}\sim1$\,ms) plus, for the eight originally DM-conditioned
pairs, the residual dispersive delay left by the CHIME--DSA DM offset. With the
new phase-coherence measurements, the largest band offset is
$0.117\,\mathrm{pc\,cm^{-3}}$ (FRB~20221113A), corresponding to approximately
$2.8$\,ms when re-referenced between 1.5\,GHz and 400\,MHz,
a convention-dependent term reported separately rather than folded in. We
assign each pair a $1\sigma$ timing budget $\sigma_i$ by combining, in
quadrature, the shared-DM combined CHIME+DSA arrival error, the intrinsic pulse

source:
cd /Users/jakobfaber/Developer/repos/github.com/jakobtfaber/Faber2026 2>/dev/null
sed -n '275,300p' sections/toa.tex
stdout:
quadrature, the shared-DM combined CHIME+DSA arrival error, the intrinsic pulse
width, and the $\sigma_{\rm clock}\sim1$\,ms term, and accept a pair when its
residual is consistent with the geometric delay to within $|\Delta t_i| \le
3\sigma_i$. The budget is dominated by the pulse width for the two broadest
bursts, so the largest raw residual---$+8.4$\,ms for FRB~20220506D, a $74$\,ms-wide
burst---is only $0.1\sigma$. All twelve residuals ($-2.7$ to $+8.4$\,ms,
tabulated with their $\sigma_i$ in Table~\ref{tab:sample}) satisfy the criterion;
the tightest is FRB~20221203A at $2.6\sigma$. A residual of several tens of ms
for a narrow, well-localized burst---inconsistent with both its few-ms budget and
the $\lesssim\!4.5$\,ms geometric delay---would fail.
Because nine of the twelve residuals are positive, we also test for a
constant inter-site timing offset: the inverse-variance-weighted mean
residual is $+2.1\pm0.9$\,ms ($2.4\sigma$ from zero), a network-level shift
comparable to the $\sigma_{\rm clock}\sim1$\,ms per-site timestamp systematic
already carried in the budget, and a constant-offset model is only mildly
preferred over zero offset ($\chi^{2}=8.9/11$ versus $14.7/12$). Subtracting
the fitted offset leaves every pair within $1.8\sigma$ of the geometric
prediction (maximum $2.6\sigma$ without subtraction), so the acceptance
verdicts are unchanged in either convention; we retain the unsubtracted
residuals in Table~\ref{tab:sample} and flag the weighted mean as a candidate
inter-site calibration term for the archival release. We stress
that the association verdict does not rest on the timing residual alone: the
decisive gates are the chance-coincidence probability, positional coincidence,
and (for the eight bursts with a pre-specified DM-conditioned association
term) DM agreement, for which the residual is a
supporting consistency diagnostic.

# [edit_file] /Users/jakobfaber/Developer/repos/github.com/jakobtfaber/Faber2026/sections/toa.tex
--- /Users/jakobfaber/Developer/repos/github.com/jakobtfaber/Faber2026/sections/toa.tex
+++ /Users/jakobfaber/Developer/repos/github.com/jakobtfaber/Faber2026/sections/toa.tex
-supporting consistency diagnostic.
+supporting consistency diagnostic.
+
+The mixed sign of the referenced offsets---seven pairs with the CHIME arrival
+formally earlier and five with the DSA arrival earlier once both are referred to
+400\,MHz---is a consequence of this decomposition rather than a tension with it
+(Figure~\ref{fig:toa-offset-decomposition}). The geometric term supplies a
+near-constant $-2.17\,$ms pedestal (spread $0.26\,$ms across the twelve
+sightlines, because the sources cluster in declination near CHIME transit so the
+DRAO--OVRO baseline projects almost identically), and cannot by itself reverse
+the order. The sign is instead set by the residual, whose $\sim\!10\,$ms
+sample spread corresponds to only $\approx1.1\,\mathrm{pc\,cm^{-3}}$ of
+equivalent shared-DM offset ($\mathrm{d}\Delta t/\mathrm{d}\mathrm{DM}=
+-9.41\,\mathrm{ms\,pc^{-1}\,cm^{3}}$; Section~\ref{sec:toa-definition})---i.e.\
+sub-$\mathrm{pc\,cm^{-3}}$ differences between each sightline's true dispersion
+and the adopted shared DM, plus intrinsic band-to-band emission-time structure.
+Crucially, the arrival order and its sign are \emph{not} inputs to any
+association gate: the chance-coincidence window ($\pm1\,$s) is larger than the
+largest referenced offset ($5.8\,$ms for FRB~20240203A) by more than two orders
+of magnitude, so a sign flip of a few milliseconds is invisible to
+$P_{\rm cc}$. An unrelated interloper would exhibit the same few-millisecond
+scatter; what a chance pair would \emph{not} reproduce is the shared geometric
+pedestal, which is therefore a positive association signature rather than a
+liability.
